Section.Text: The Minister may enter into an agreement with the government of any province, or an Indigenous governing body, for the purpose of obtaining information in connection with the administration or enforcement of this Act or the regulations and of furnishing to that government, or Indigenous governing body, under prescribed conditions, any information obtained by or on behalf of the Minister in the course of the administration or enforcement of this Act or the regulations, if the Minister is satisfied that the information to be furnished to that government, or Indigenous governing body, under the agreement is to be used for the purpose of the administration of a social program, income assistance program or health insurance program in the province or of the Indigenous governing body.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
Paragraph 13(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: providing for the suspension of payment of a special allowance during any investigation respecting the eligibility of a department, agency, institution or Indigenous governing body to receive the special allowance and specifying the circumstances in which payment of a special allowance, the payment of which has been suspended, may be resumed;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 13(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: specifying for the purposes of this Act the circumstances in which a child shall be considered to be maintained by a department, agency, institution or Indigenous governing body; and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.

Section.Text: Paragraph 211(6)(e) of the Excise Act, 2001 is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (ix), by adding “or” at the end of subparagraph (x) and by adding the following after subparagraph (x):

Subparagraph.Label: (xi)
Subparagraph.Text: to an official of the Agency solely for the purpose of the collection of amounts owing to Her Majesty in right of Canada under the Canada Emergency Business Account program established by Export Development Canada in accordance with an authorization made under subsection 23(1) of the Export Development Act;

Subsection.Text: Section 1100 of the Income Tax Regulations is amended by adding the following before subsection (1):
For the purposes of paragraph 20(1)(a) of the Act, a deduction is allowed in computing an eligible person or partnership’s income for each taxation year equal to the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the eligible person or partnership’s immediate expensing limit for the taxation year,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the undepreciated capital cost to the eligible person or partnership as of the end of the taxation year (before making any deduction under this Part for the taxation year) of property that is designated immediate expensing property for the taxation year, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the eligible person or partnership is not a Canadian-controlled private corporation, the amount of income, if any, earned from the source of income that is a business or property (computed without regard to paragraph 20(1)(a) of the Act) in which the relevant designated immediate expensing property is used for the eligible person or partnership’s taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Before computing any other deduction permitted under this Part and Schedules II to VI, the amount of any deduction made under subsection (0.1) by an eligible person or partnership in respect of a designated immediate expensing property of a prescribed class shall be deducted from the undepreciated capital cost of the particular class to which the property belongs.

For the purposes of paragraph (0.1)(b), in respect of property of a class in Schedule II that is immediate expensing property of an eligible person or partnership solely because of subparagraph (c)(i) of the definition immediate expensing property in subsection 1104(3.1), amounts incurred by any person or partnership in respect of the property are not to be included in determining the undepreciated capital cost to the eligible person or partnership as of the end of the taxation year (before making any deduction under this Part for the taxation year) of property that is designated immediate expensing property for the taxation year if the amounts are incurred before April 19, 2021 (if the eligible person or partnership is a Canadian-controlled private corporation) or before 2022 (if the eligible person or partnership is an individual or Canadian partnership), unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the property was acquired by an eligible person or partnership from another person or partnership (referred to in this paragraph as the “transferee” and the “transferor”, respectively)

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the transferee is a Canadian-controlled private corporation, after April 18, 2021, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the transferee is an individual or a Canadian partnership, after December 31, 2021;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transferee was either

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the eligible person or partnership, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person or partnership that does not deal at arm’s length with the eligible person or partnership; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: dealt at arm’s length with the transferee, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: held the property as inventory.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 1100(1.1) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Despite subsections (0.1), (1) and (3), the amount deductible by a taxpayer for a taxation year in respect of a property that is a specified leasing property at the end of the year is the lesser of
Subsection 1100(1.12) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Despite subsections (0.1), (1) and (1.1), where, in a taxation year, a taxpayer has acquired a property that was not used by the taxpayer for any purpose in that year and the first use of the property by the taxpayer is a lease of the property in respect of which subsection (1.1) applies, the amount allowed to the taxpayer under subsections (0.1) and (1) in respect of the property for the year shall be deemed to be nil.
The portion of subsection 1100(11) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Despite subsections (0.1) and (1), in no case shall the aggregate of deductions, each of which is a deduction in respect of property of a prescribed class owned by a taxpayer that includes rental property owned by him, otherwise allowed to the taxpayer by virtue of subsection (0.1) or (1) in computing his income for a taxation year, exceed the amount, if any, by which
The portion of subsection 1100(15) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Despite subsections (0.1) and (1), in no case shall the aggregate of deductions, each of which is a deduction in respect of property of a prescribed class that is leasing property owned by a taxpayer, otherwise allowed to the taxpayer under subsection (0.1) or (1) in computing his income for a taxation year, exceed the amount, if any, by which
The portion of subsection 1100(20.1) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The total of all amounts each of which is a deduction in respect of computer tax shelter property allowed to the taxpayer under subsection (0.1) or (1) in computing a taxpayer’s income for a taxation year shall not exceed the amount, if any, by which
Subsection 1100(21.1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Despite subsections (0.1) and (1), where a taxpayer has acquired property described in paragraph (s) of Class 10 in Schedule II, or in paragraph (m) of Class 12 of Schedule II, the deduction in respect of the property otherwise allowed to the taxpayer under subsection (0.1) or (1) in computing the taxpayer’s income for a taxation year shall not exceed the amount that it would be if the capital cost to the taxpayer of the property were reduced by the portion of any debt obligation of the taxpayer outstanding at the end of the year that is convertible into an interest or, for civil law, a right in the property or an interest in the taxpayer.
The portion of subsection 1100(24) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Despite subsections (0.1) and (1), in no case shall the total of deductions, each of which is a deduction in respect of property of Class 34, 43.1, 43.2, 47 or 48 in Schedule II that is specified energy property owned by a taxpayer, otherwise allowed to the taxpayer under subsection (0.1) or (1) in computing the taxpayer’s income for a taxation year, exceed the amount, if any, by which
Subsections (1) to (8) are deemed to have come into force on April 19, 2021.
Subsection 1102(20.1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsections 1100(0.3) and (2.02) and 1104(3.1) and (4), a particular person or partnership and another person or partnership shall be considered not to be dealing at arm’s length with each other in respect of the acquisition or ownership of a property if, in the absence of this subsection, they would be considered to be dealing at arm’s length with each other and it may reasonably be considered that the principal purpose of any transaction or event, or a series of transactions or events, is to cause

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the property to qualify as accelerated investment incentive property or immediate expensing property; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the particular person or partnership and the other person or partnership to satisfy the condition in subclause 1100(2.02)(a)(i)(C)(I) or subparagraph 1100(0.3)(c)(i).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 19, 2021.
Section 1104 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
The following definitions apply in this Part and Schedules II to VI.

Definition.Text: designated immediate expensing property for a taxation year, means property of an eligible person or partnership that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is immediate expensing property of the eligible person or partnership;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: became available for use by the eligible person or partnership in the taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is designated as designated immediate expensing property in prescribed form filed by the eligible person or partnership with the Minister for the taxation year

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the eligible person or partnership is a partnership, on or before the day that is 12 months after the day on which any member of the partnership is required to file an information return under section 229 for the fiscal period to which the designation relates, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, on or before the day that is 12 months after the eligible person or partnership’s filing-due date for the taxation year to which the designation relates. (bien relatif à la passation en charges immédiate désigné)

Definition.Text: eligible person or partnership for a taxation year, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation that was a Canadian-controlled private corporation throughout the year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an individual (other than a trust) who was resident in Canada throughout the year; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a Canadian partnership all of the members of which were, throughout the period, persons described in paragraph (a) or (b). (personne ou société de personnes admissible)

Definition.Text: immediate expensing property for a taxation year, means property of a prescribed class (other than property included in any of Classes 1 to 6, 14.1, 17, 47, 49 and 51 in Schedule II) of an eligible person or partnership that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is acquired by the eligible person or partnership

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the eligible person or partnership is a Canadian-controlled private corporation, after April 18, 2021, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the eligible person or partnership is an individual or a Canadian partnership, after December 31, 2021;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: becomes available for use

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the eligible person or partnership is an individual or a Canadian partnership all the members of which are individuals throughout the taxation year, before 2025, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, before 2024; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: meets either of the following conditions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the property

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: has not been used for any purpose before it was acquired by the eligible person or partnership, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is not a property in respect of which an amount has been deducted under paragraph 20(1)(a) or subsection 20(16) of the Act by any person or partnership for a taxation year ending before the time the property was acquired by the eligible person or partnership, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the property was not

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: acquired in circumstances where

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the eligible person or partnership was deemed to have been allowed or deducted an amount under paragraph 20(1)(a) of the Act in respect of the property in computing income for previous taxation years, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: the undepreciated capital cost of depreciable property of a prescribed class of the eligible person or partnership was reduced by an amount determined by reference to the amount by which the capital cost of the property to the eligible person or partnership exceeds its cost amount, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: previously owned or acquired by the eligible person or partnership or by a person or partnership with which the eligible person or partnership did not deal at arm’s length at any time when the property was owned or acquired by the person or partnership. (bien relatif à la passation en charges immédiate)

Definition.Text: taxpayer unless the context otherwise requires, includes an eligible person or partnership. (contribuable)

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Part and Schedules II to VI, an eligible person or partnership’s immediate expensing limit for a taxation year is $1,500,000 unless the eligible person or partnership is associated (within the meaning of section 256 of the Act, as modified by subsection (3.6)) in the taxation year with one or more other eligible persons or partnerships, in which case, except as otherwise provided in this section, its immediate expensing limit is nil.

Despite subsection (3.2), if all the eligible persons or partnerships that are associated with each other (within the meaning of section 256 of the Act, as modified by subsection (3.6)) in a taxation year file with the Minister in prescribed form an agreement that assigns for the purpose of this Part and Schedules II to VI a percentage to one or more of them for the year, the immediate expensing limit for the year of each of the eligible persons or partnerships is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the total of the percentages assigned in the agreement does not exceed 100%, $1,500,000 multiplied by the percentage assigned to that eligible person or partnership in the agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, nil.

Subsection.Text: If any of the eligible persons or partnerships that are associated with each other (within the meaning of section 256 of the Act, as modified by subsection (3.6)) in a taxation year has failed to file with the Minister an agreement described in subsection (3.3) within 30 days after notice in writing by the Minister has been forwarded to any of them that such an agreement is required for the purpose of any assessment of tax under Part I of the Act, the Minister shall, for the purpose of this Part and Schedules II to VI, allocate an amount to one or more of them for the taxation year.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Special rules for immediate expensing limit
Subsection.Text: Despite subsections (3.2) to (3.4),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: where an eligible person or partnership (in this paragraph referred to as the “first person”) has more than one taxation year ending in the same calendar year and it is associated (within the meaning of section 256 of the Act, as modified by subsection (3.6)) in two or more of those taxation years with another eligible person or partnership (in this paragraph referred to as the “other person”) that has a taxation year ending in that calendar year, the immediate expensing limit of the first person for each taxation year ending in the calendar year in which it is associated (within the meaning of section 256 of the Act, as modified by subsection (3.6)) with the other person that ends after the first such taxation year ending in that calendar year is, subject to the application of paragraph (b), an amount equal to the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: its immediate expensing limit determined under subsection (3.3) or (3.4) for the first such taxation year ending in the calendar year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: its immediate expensing limit determined under subsection (3.3) or (3.4) for the particular taxation year ending in the calendar year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where an eligible person or partnership has a taxation year that is less than 51 weeks, its immediate expensing limit for the year is that proportion of its immediate expensing limit for the year determined without reference to this paragraph that the number of days in the year is of 365.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Part and Schedules II to VI, in determining whether an eligible person or partnership is associated (within the meaning of section 256 of the Act, as modified by this subsection) with another eligible person or partnership in a taxation year

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the eligible person or partnership is a partnership,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the partnership is deemed to be a corporation (in this subsection referred to as a “deemed corporation”) for the year,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the deemed corporation is deemed to have a capital stock of a single class of shares, with a total of 100 issued and outstanding shares,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: each member (in this subsection referred to as a “deemed shareholder”) of the deemed corporation is deemed to be a shareholder of the deemed corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: each deemed shareholder of the deemed corporation is deemed to hold a number of shares in the capital stock of the deemed corporation determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is equal to

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the deemed shareholder’s specified proportion for the last fiscal period of the deemed corporation, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the deemed shareholder does not have a specified proportion described in clause (A), the proportion that the fair market value of the deemed shareholder’s interest in the deemed corporation at that time is of the fair market value of all interests in the deemed corporation at that time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the deemed corporation’s fiscal period is deemed to be its taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the eligible person or partnership is an individual (other than a trust) who carries on a business or has acquired immediate expensing property

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the individual, in respect of that business or property, is deemed to be a corporation that is controlled by the individual, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the corporation’s taxation year is deemed to be the same as the individual’s taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 19, 2021.
The definitions biogas and producer gas in subsection 1104(13) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: biogas means the gas produced by the anaerobic digestion of specified waste material. (biogaz)
producer gas means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a property of a taxpayer that becomes available for use by the taxpayer before 2025, fuel the composition of which, excluding its water content, is all or substantially all non-condensable gases that is generated primarily from eligible waste fuel or specified waste material using a thermo-chemical conversion process and that is not generated from any feedstock other than eligible waste fuel, specified waste material or fossil fuel; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a property of a taxpayer that becomes available for use by the taxpayer after 2024, fuel

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the composition of which, excluding its water content, is all or substantially all non-condensable gases,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: that is generated using a thermo-chemical conversion process,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: that is generated from feedstock of which no more than 25% is fossil fuel when measured in terms of energy content (expressed as a higher heating value of the feedstock), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: that is not generated from any feedstock other than eligible waste fuel, specified waste material or fossil fuel. (gaz de gazéification)

Subsection.Text: The definitions plant residue and separated organics in subsection 1104(13) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: plant residue means residue of plants (not including wood waste and waste that no longer has the chemical properties of the plants of which it is a residue) that would otherwise be waste material. (résidus végétaux)

separated organics means organic waste (other than waste that is considered to be toxic or hazardous waste under any law of Canada or a province) that could be disposed of in an eligible waste management facility or eligible landfill site. (matières organiques séparées)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1104(13) of the Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: gaseous biofuel means a fuel produced all or substantially all from specified waste material that is a gas at a temperature of 15.6°C (60°F) and a pressure of 101 kPa (14.7 psia). (biocarburants gazeux)

liquid biofuel means a fuel produced all or substantially all from specified waste material or carbon dioxide that is a liquid at a temperature of 15.6°C (60°F) and a pressure of 101 kPa (14.7 psia). (biocarburants liquides)

solid biofuel means a fuel produced all or substantially all from specified waste material that is a solid at a temperature of 15.6°C (60°F) and a pressure of 101 kPa (14.7 psia) (other than charcoal that is used for cooking or fuels with fossil fuel-derived ignition accelerants) and that has undergone

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a thermo-chemical conversion process to increase its carbon fraction and densification; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: densification into pellets or briquettes. (biocarburants solides)

Definition.Text: specified waste material means wood waste, plant residue, municipal waste, sludge from an eligible sewage treatment facility, spent pulping liquor, food and animal waste, manure, pulp and paper by-product and separated organics. (déchets déterminés)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of property acquired after April 18, 2021 that has not been used or acquired for use before April 19, 2021.
Subsections (2) and (3) are deemed to have come into force on April 19, 2021.
Clause 1104(17)(a)(ii)(A) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: any of subparagraphs (d)(vii) to (ix), (xi), (xiii), (xiv), (xvi), (xvii) and (xix) to (xxii) of Class 43.1, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to property acquired after April 18, 2021 that has not been used or acquired for use before April 19, 2021.

Section.Text: Section 1106 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Heading.TitleText: COVID-19 — Application for a Certificate of Completion

Subsection.Text: In respect of applications filed with the Minister of Canadian Heritage in respect of film or video productions for which the labour expenditure of the corporation in respect of the production for the taxation years ending in 2020 or 2021 was greater than nil, the definition application for a certificate of completion in subsection (1) is to be read as follows:

Definition.Text: application for a certificate of completion, in respect of a film or video production, means an application by a prescribed taxable Canadian corporation in respect of the production, filed with the Minister of Canadian Heritage before the day (in this Division referred to as “the production’s application deadline”) that is the later of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day that is 24 months after the end of the corporation’s taxation year in which the production’s principal photography began,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day that is 18 months after the day referred to in paragraph (a), if the corporation has filed, with the Canada Revenue Agency, and provided to the Minister of Canadian Heritage a copy of, a waiver described in subparagraph 152(4)(a)(ii) of the Act, within the normal reassessment period for the corporation in respect of the first and second taxation years ending after the production’s principal photography began, or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the day that is 12 months after the day referred to in paragraph (b), if the corporation has filed, with the Canada Revenue Agency, and provided to the Minister of Canadian Heritage a copy of, a waiver described in subparagraph 152(4)(a)(ii) of the Act, within the normal reassessment period for the corporation in respect of the first, second and third taxation years ending after the production’s principal photography began. (demande de certificat d’achèvement)

Subsection.Text: The reference to “2-year period” in subparagraph (a)(iv) of the definition excluded production in subsection (1) is to be read as a reference to “three-year period” in respect of film or video productions for which the labour expenditure of the corporation in respect of the production for the taxation years ending in 2020 or 2021 was greater than nil.

Section.Text: The Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 3702:
For the purpose of subsection 149.1(14) of the Act, the following is prescribed information for the public information return of a charity in a taxation year:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of each grantee organization that received total qualifying disbursements from the charity in excess of $5,000 in the taxation year, the name of the grantee organization;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the purpose of each qualifying disbursement made to a grantee organization referred to in paragraph (a) in the taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the total amount disbursed by the charity to each grantee organization referred to in paragraph (a) in the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Section 5202 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: qualified zero-emission technology manufacturing activities means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: qualified activities that are

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: performed in connection with the manufacturing or processing of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: solar energy conversion equipment, including solar thermal collectors, photovoltaic solar arrays and custom supporting structures or frames, but excluding passive solar heating equipment,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: wind energy conversion equipment, including wind turbine towers, nacelles and rotor blades,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: water energy conversion equipment, including hydroelectric, water current, tidal and wave energy conversion equipment,

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: geothermal energy equipment,

Clause.Label: (E)
Clause.Text: equipment for a ground source heat pump system,

Clause.Label: (F)
Clause.Text: electrical energy storage equipment used for storage of renewable energy or for providing grid-scale storage or other ancillary services, including battery, compressed air and flywheel storage systems,

Clause.Label: (G)
Clause.Text: equipment used to charge, or to dispense hydrogen to, property included in clause (J),

Clause.Label: (H)
Clause.Text: equipment used for the production of hydrogen by electrolysis of water,

Clause.Label: (I)
Clause.Text: equipment that is a component of property included in clauses (A) to (H), if such equipment is purpose-built or designed exclusively to form an integral part of that property,

Clause.Label: (J)
Clause.Text: property that

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: would be a zero-emission vehicle (as defined in subsection 248(1) of the Act if that definition were read without reference to its paragraphs (b) and (c)), or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: is described in subparagraph (a)(i) of Class 56 of Schedule II, and

Clause.Label: (K)
Clause.Text: integral components of the powertrain of property included in clause (J), including batteries or fuel cells, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: not the manufacturing or processing of general purpose components or equipment which components or equipment are suitable for integration into property other than property described in subparagraph (i);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: qualified activities that are performed in connection with production in Canada of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: hydrogen by electrolysis of water,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: gaseous biofuel (as defined in subsection 1104(13)),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: liquid biofuel (as defined in subsection 1104(13)), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: solid biofuel (as defined in subsection 1104(13)); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the conversion of a vehicle, performed in Canada, into a property described in clause (a)(i)(J); (activités admissibles de fabrication de technologies à zéro émission)

Definition.Text: ZETM cost of capital, of a corporation for a taxation year, means the portion of the cost of capital of the corporation for the year that reflects the extent to which each property included in the calculation of the cost of capital was used directly in qualified zero-emission technology manufacturing activities of the corporation during the year; (coût en capital de FTZE)
ZETM cost of labour, of a corporation for a taxation year, means the portion of the cost of labour of the corporation for that year that reflects the extent to which

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the salaries and wages included in the calculation of the cost of labour were paid or payable to persons for the portion of their time that they were directly engaged in qualified zero-emission technology manufacturing activities of the corporation during the year, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the other amounts included in the calculation of the cost of labour were paid or payable to persons for the performance of functions that would be directly related to qualified zero-emission technology manufacturing activities of the corporation during the year if those persons were employees of the corporation; (coût en main-d’œuvre de FTZE)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
The portion of section 5204 of the Regulations before the definition cost of capital is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: If a corporation is a member of a partnership at any time in a taxation year of the corporation, the following definitions apply:

Subsection.Text: Section 5204 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: ZETM cost of capital, of the corporation for the year, means the portion of the cost of capital of the corporation for that year that reflects the extent to which each property included in the calculation of the cost of capital was used directly in qualified zero-emission technology manufacturing activities

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: of the corporation during the year, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of the partnership during its fiscal period coinciding with or ending in the year, as the case may be; (coût en capital de FTZE)

Definition.Text: ZETM cost of labour, of the corporation for the year, means the portion of the cost of labour of the corporation for that year that reflects the extent to which

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the salaries and wages included in the calculation of the cost of labour were paid or payable to persons for the portion of their time that they were directly engaged in qualified zero-emission technology manufacturing activities

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: of the corporation during the year, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: of the partnership during its fiscal period coinciding with or ending in the year, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the other amounts included in the calculation of the cost of labour were paid or payable to persons for the performance of functions that would be directly related to qualified zero-emission technology manufacturing activities of the corporation during the year, or of the partnership during its fiscal period coinciding with or ending in the year, if those persons were employees of the corporation or the partnership, as the case may be; (coût en main-d’œuvre de FTZE)

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.

Section.Text: Section 9300 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: The references to “24 months” in paragraphs 9300(1)(a) and (b) are to be read as references to “36 months” in respect of film or video productions for which the Canadian labour expenditure of the corporation in respect of the production for the taxation years ending in 2020 or 2021 was greater than nil.
Subparagraphs (c)(i) and (ii) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: is used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, to generate electrical energy, or both electrical and heat energy, using only fuel that is eligible waste fuel, fossil fuel, producer gas, spent pulping liquor or any combination of those fuels,

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: if the system is rated to generate more than three megawatts of electrical energy, meets the following condition on an annual basis:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is 11,000 BTU per kilowatt-hour,
is the energy content of fossil fuel other than solution gas (expressed as the higher heating value of the fuel) consumed by the system in BTU,
is the energy content of the eligible waste fuel, producer gas and spent pulping liquor (expressed as the higher heating value of the fuel) consumed by the system in BTU,
is the gross electrical energy produced by the system in kilowatt-hours, and
is the net useful energy in the form of heat exported from the system to a thermal host in BTU, and

Provision.Label: (C)
Provision.Text: uses fuel of which no more than 25% of the energy content (expressed as the higher heating value of the fuel) is from fossil fuel, as determined on an annual basis, or

Subsection.Text: Clause (d)(i)(B) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: it is not a building, part of a building (other than a solar collector that is not a window and that is integrated into a building), energy equipment that backs up equipment described in subclause (A)(I) or (II) nor equipment that distributes heated or cooled air or water in a building,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(iv) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (iv)
Provision.Text: heat recovery equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily for the purpose of conserving energy, reducing the requirement to acquire energy or extracting heat for sale, by extracting for reuse thermal waste that is generated directly in an industrial process (other than an industrial process that generates or processes electrical energy), including such equipment that consists of heat exchange equipment, compressors used to upgrade low pressure steam, vapour or gas, waste heat boilers and other ancillary equipment such as control panels, fans, instruments or pumps, but not including property that is employed in re-using the recovered heat (such as property that is part of the internal heating or cooling system of a building or electrical generating equipment) or is a building,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(vii) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (vii)
Provision.Text: equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily for the purpose of generating electrical energy or heat energy, or both electrical and heat energy, solely from geothermal energy, including such equipment that consists of piping (including above or below ground piping and the cost of completing a well (including the wellhead and production string), or trenching, for the purpose of installing that piping), pumps, heat exchangers, steam separators, electrical generating equipment and ancillary equipment used to collect the geothermal heat, but not including buildings, distribution equipment, equipment described in subclause (i)(A)(II), property otherwise included in Class 10 and property that would be included in Class 17 if that Class were read without reference to its paragraph (a.1),

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(ix) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: is used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, for the sole purpose of generating heat energy, not using any fuel other than eligible waste fuel, fossil fuel, producer gas or a combination of those fuels,

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: uses fuel of which no more than 25% of the energy content (expressed as the higher heating value of the fuel) is from fossil fuel, as determined on an annual basis,

Provision.Label: (I)
Provision.Text: fuel handling equipment used to upgrade the combustible portion of the fuel,

Provision.Label: (II)
Provision.Text: control, feedwater and condensate systems, and

Provision.Label: (I)
Provision.Text: equipment used for the purpose of producing heat energy to operate electrical generating equipment,

Provision.Label: (III)
Provision.Text: heat rejection equipment (such as condensers and cooling water systems),

Provision.Label: (VI)
Provision.Text: property otherwise included in Class 10 or 17,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs (d)(xi) and (xii) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (xi)
Provision.Text: equipment all or substantially all of the use of which by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, is to produce liquid biofuel, including storage, materials handling and ash-handling equipment and equipment used to remove non-combustibles and contaminants from the fuels produced, but not including

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: equipment used to produce spent pulping liquor,

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: equipment used for the collection or transportation of specified waste material or carbon dioxide,

Provision.Label: (C)
Provision.Text: equipment used for the transmission or distribution of liquid biofuel,

Provision.Label: (D)
Provision.Text: property that would otherwise be included in Class 17,

Provision.Label: (xii)
Provision.Text: fixed location fuel cell equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, that uses hydrogen generated only from ancillary electrolysis equipment (or, if the fuel cell is reversible, the fuel cell itself) using electricity all or substantially all of which is generated by using kinetic energy of flowing water or wave or tidal energy or by geothermal, photovoltaic, wind energy conversion, or hydro-electric equipment, of the taxpayer or the lessee, and equipment ancillary to the fuel cell equipment other than buildings or other structures, transmission equipment, distribution equipment, auxiliary electrical generating equipment and property otherwise included in Class 10 or 17,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(xiv) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (xiv)
Provision.Text: property that is used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily for the purpose of generating electricity using kinetic energy of flowing water or wave or tidal energy, including support structures, control and conditioning equipment, submerged cables and transmission equipment, but not including buildings, distribution equipment, auxiliary electricity generating equipment, property otherwise included in Class 10 and property that would be included in Class 17 if that class were read without reference to its subparagraph (a.1)(i),

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(xvi) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (xvi)
Provision.Text: equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily for the purpose of generating producer gas (other than producer gas that is to be converted into liquid fuels or chemicals), including related piping (including fans and compressors), air separation equipment, storage equipment, equipment used for drying or shredding feedstock, ash-handling equipment, equipment used to upgrade the producer gas into biomethane and equipment used to remove non-combustibles and contaminants from the producer gas, but not including, buildings or other structures, heat rejection equipment (such as condensers and cooling water systems), equipment used to convert producer gas into liquid fuels or chemicals, and property otherwise included in Class 10 or 17,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(xvi) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: is used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily for the purpose of generating producer gas (other than producer gas that is to be converted into liquid fuels or chemicals),

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: uses feedstock of which no more than 25% of the energy content (expressed as the higher heating value of the feedstock) is from fossil fuel, as determined on an annual basis,

Provision.Label: (IV)
Provision.Text: equipment used for drying or shredding feedstock,

Provision.Label: (VI)
Provision.Text: equipment used to upgrade the producer gas into biomethane, and

Provision.Label: (VII)
Provision.Text: equipment used to remove non-combustibles and contaminants from the producer gas, and

Provision.Label: (II)
Provision.Text: heat rejection equipment (such as condensers and cooling water systems),

Provision.Label: (III)
Provision.Text: equipment used to convert producer gas into liquid fuels or chemicals, and

Provision.Label: (IV)
Provision.Text: property otherwise included in Class 10 or 17,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (xvii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (xviii):

Provision.Label: (xix)
Provision.Text: a pumped hydroelectric energy storage installation all or substantially all of the use of which by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, is to store electrical energy including reversing turbines, transmission equipment, dams, reservoirs and related structures, and that meets the condition in either subclause (d)(xviii)(B)(I) or (II) in this Class, but not including

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: property used solely for backup electrical energy, and

Provision.Label: (xx)
Provision.Text: equipment all or substantially all of the use of which by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, is to produce solid biofuel, including storage, materials handling and ash-handling equipment, but not including

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: equipment used to make wood chips, hog fuel or black liquor,

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: property that would otherwise be included in Class 17,

Provision.Label: (xxi)
Provision.Text: equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, to dispense hydrogen for use in automotive equipment powered by hydrogen, including vaporization, compression, cooling and storage equipment, but not including

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: equipment used for the production or transmission of hydrogen,

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: equipment used for the transmission or distribution of electricity,

Provision.Label: (xxii)
Provision.Text: equipment all or substantially all of the use of which by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, is to produce hydrogen through electrolysis of water, including electrolysers, rectifiers and other ancillary electrical equipment, water treatment and conditioning equipment and equipment used for hydrogen compression and storage, but not including

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: equipment used for the transmission or distribution of hydrogen,

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: equipment used for the transmission or distribution of electricity,

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1), (5) and (9) apply in respect of property of a taxpayer that becomes available for use by the taxpayer after 2024.
Subsections (2) to (4), (6) to (8) and (10) apply to property acquired after April 18, 2021 that has not been used or acquired for use before April 19, 2021.
Paragraph (a) of Class 43.2 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: otherwise than because of paragraph (d) of that Class; or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b)(i) of Class 43.2 in Schedule II to the Regulations is repealed.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply in respect of property of a taxpayer that becomes available for use by the taxpayer after 2024.
The definition applicant in section 2 of the Children’s Special Allowance Regulations is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: applicant means a department, agency, institution or Indigenous governing body referred to in subsection 3(1) of the Act; (demandeur)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
The portion of section 7 of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: The information referred to in section 11 of the Act may be furnished to the government of a province or to an Indigenous governing body, under the terms of an agreement between the Minister and that government or Indigenous governing body, for the purpose of the administration of a social, income assistance or health insurance program of that province or Indigenous governing body that is specified in the agreement, on condition that

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
Paragraphs 9(a) and (b) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the applicant, at the end of the month, provides for the child’s care, maintenance, education, training and advancement to a greater extent than any other department, agency, institution, Indigenous governing body or any person; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the applicant is an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 3(1)(a) to (c) of the Act that has applied in respect of a child who

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: was formerly in the care of foster parents or was formerly maintained by an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 3(1)(a) to (c) of the Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has been placed in the permanent or temporary custody of a guardian, tutor or other individual occupying a similar role for the month, under a decree, order or judgment of a competent tribunal, or under the laws of an Indigenous governing body, who has received financial assistance from the applicant for the month in respect of the child’s maintenance.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.

Section.Text: If Bill C-222, introduced in the 1st session of the 44th Parliament and entitled An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (travel expenses deduction for tradespersons), receives royal assent before or on the same day as this Act receives royal assent, then, on the day this Act receives royal assent, that Act is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.

If Bill C-241, introduced in the 1st session of the 44th Parliament and entitled An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (deduction of travel expenses for tradespersons), receives royal assent before or on the same day as this Act receives royal assent, then, on the day this Act receives royal assent, that Act is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.

If Bill S-216, introduced in the 1st session of the 44th Parliament and entitled An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (use of resources of a registered charity), receives royal assent before or on the same day as this Act receives royal assent, then, on the day this Act receives royal assent, that Act is deemed never to come into force and is repealed.

Heading.Label: PART 2
Heading.TitleText: Amendments to the Excise Tax Act (GST/HST Measures)

Subsection.Text: The Excise Tax Act is amended by adding the following after section 192:

Section.Text: If a taxable supply by way of sale of a single unit residential complex (as defined in subsection 254(1)) or of a residential condominium unit is made in Canada under an agreement of purchase and sale (in this section referred to as the “purchase agreement”) entered into with a builder of the single unit residential complex or of the residential condominium unit and if another supply by way of assignment of the purchase agreement is made by a person (other than the builder) under another agreement, then the following rules apply for the purposes of this Part:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the other supply is deemed to be a taxable supply, by way of sale, of real property that is an interest in the single unit residential complex or residential condominium unit; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the consideration for the other supply is deemed to be equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the consideration for the other supply as otherwise determined for the purposes of this Part, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the other agreement indicates in writing that a part of the consideration for the other supply is attributable to the reimbursement of a deposit paid under the purchase agreement, the part of the consideration for the other supply, as otherwise determined for the purposes of this Part, that is solely attributable to the reimbursement of the deposit paid under the purchase agreement, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, zero.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of any supply by way of assignment of an agreement of purchase and sale if the supply is made after May 6, 2022.
Clause (a)(ii)(C) of the definition facility supply in subsection 259(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: a nurse practitioner acting in the course of the practise of a nurse practitioner, or

Subsection.Text: Clause (a)(iii)(B) of the definition facility supply in subsection 259(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a physician or nurse practitioner be at, or be on-call to attend at, the public hospital or qualifying facility at all times when the individual is at the public hospital or qualifying facility,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (a)(ii) of the definition home medical supply in subsection 259(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: after a physician acting in the course of the practise of medicine, a nurse practitioner acting in the course of the practise of a nurse practitioner or a prescribed person acting in prescribed circumstances has identified or confirmed that it is appropriate for the process to take place at the individual’s place of residence or lodging (other than a public hospital or a qualifying facility),

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition home medical supply in subsection 259(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of which the property is made available, or the service is rendered, to the individual at the individual’s place of residence or lodging (other than a public hospital or a qualifying facility), on the authorization of a person who is responsible for coordinating the process and under circumstances in which it is reasonable to expect that the person will carry out that responsibility in consultation with, or with ongoing reference to instructions for the process given by, a physician acting in the course of the practise of medicine, a nurse practitioner acting in the course of the practise of a nurse practitioner or a prescribed person acting in prescribed circumstances,

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (4) apply for the purposes of determining a rebate of a person under section 259 of the Act for claim periods ending after April 7, 2022, except that, in determining a rebate of a person for the claim period that includes April 7, 2022, the rebate is to be determined as if those subsections did not apply in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an amount of tax that became payable by the person on or before April 7, 2022;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an amount that is deemed to have been paid or collected by the person on or before April 7, 2022; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an amount that is required to be added in determining the person’s net tax

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: as a result of a branch or division of the person becoming a small supplier division on or before April 7, 2022, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: as a result of the person ceasing to be a registrant on or before April 7, 2022.

Heading.Label: PART 3
Heading.TitleText: Amendments to the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Act and Other Related Texts

Heading.Label: DIVISION 1
Heading.TitleText: Excise Act, 2001 and Other Related Texts (Vaping Products)

Subsection.Text: The definitions container, excise stamp and manufacture in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001 are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: container, in respect of a tobacco product, a cannabis product or a vaping product, means a wrapper, package, carton, box, crate, bottle, vial or other container that contains the tobacco product, cannabis product or vaping product. (contenant)
excise stamp means a tobacco excise stamp, a cannabis excise stamp or a vaping excise stamp. (timbre d’accise)
manufacture includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a tobacco product, any step in the preparation or working up of raw leaf tobacco into the tobacco product, including packing, stemming, reconstituting, converting or packaging the raw leaf tobacco or tobacco product; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a vaping product, any step in the production of the vaping product, including inserting a vaping substance into a vaping device or packaging the vaping product. (fabrication)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition packaged in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of raw leaf tobacco, a tobacco product, a cannabis product or a vaping product, packaged in a prescribed package; or

Subsection.Text: The definition stamped in section 2 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a vaping product, that a vaping excise stamp, and all prescribed information in a prescribed format in respect of the vaping product, are stamped, impressed, printed or marked on, indented into or affixed to the vaping product or its container in the prescribed manner to indicate that duty has been paid on the vaping product. (estampillé)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition take for use in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a cannabis product or a vaping product, to consume, analyze or destroy the cannabis product or vaping product. (utilisation pour soi)

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: additional vaping duty means a duty imposed under section 158.58. (droit additionnel sur le vapotage)
immediate container, in respect of a vaping substance, means the container that is in direct contact with the vaping substance. It does not include a vaping device. (contenant immédiat)
specified vaping province means a prescribed province. (province déterminée de vapotage)
vaping device means property (other than prescribed property) that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a device that produces emissions in the form of an aerosol and is intended to be brought to the mouth for inhalation of the aerosol;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a vaping pod or another part that may be used with a device referred to in paragraph (a); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed property. (dispositif de vapotage)

Definition.Text: vaping duty means a duty imposed under section 158.57. (droit sur le vapotage)
vaping excise stamp means a stamp that is issued by the Minister under subsection 158.36(1) and that has not been cancelled under section 158.4. (timbre d’accise de vapotage)
vaping product means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a vaping substance that is not contained within a vaping device; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a vaping device that contains a vaping substance.

ContinuedDefinition.Text: It does not include a cannabis product or a tobacco product. (produit de vapotage)

Definition.Text: vaping product drug means a vaping product (other than a prescribed vaping product) that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a drug that has been assigned a drug identification number under the Food and Drug Regulations; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed vaping product. (drogue de produit de vapotage)

Definition.Text: vaping product licensee means a person that holds a vaping product licence issued under section 14. (titulaire de licence de produits de vapotage)

vaping product marking means prescribed information that is required under this Act to be printed on, or affixed to, a container of vaping products that are not required under this Act to be stamped. (mention obligatoire pour vapotage)
vaping substance means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a substance or mixture of substances, whether or not it contains nicotine, that is produced to be used, or sold for use, with a vaping device to produce emissions in the form of an aerosol; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed substance, material or thing.

ContinuedDefinition.Text: It does not include a prescribed substance, material or thing. (substance de vapotage)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 5(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of section 25.2, subsections 25.3(1), 30(1), 32(1) and 32.1(1), section 61, subsections 70(1) and 88(1), section 158.04, subsections 158.05(1) and 158.11(1) and (2), section 158.37, subsections 158.38(1) and 158.44(1) and (2), sections 230 and 231 and subsection 238.1(1), if one of two or more persons, with the knowledge and consent of the rest of them, has anything in the person’s possession, it is deemed to be in the custody and possession of each and all of them.
The portion of subsection 5(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

In this section and in section 25.2, subsections 25.3(1), 30(1), 32(1) and 32.1(1), section 61, subsections 70(1) and 88(1), section 158.04, subsections 158.05(1) and 158.11(1) and (2), section 158.37 and subsections 158.38(1), 158.44(1) and (2) and 238.1(1), possession means not only having in one’s own personal possession but also knowingly

Section.Text: Subsection 14(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (d), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a vaping product licence, authorizing the person to manufacture vaping products.

Section.Text: Subsection 19(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, on application, the Minister may issue an excise warehouse licence to a person that is not a retailer of alcohol authorizing the person to possess in their excise warehouse manufactured tobacco, cigars or vaping products that are not stamped or non-duty-paid packaged alcohol.

Section.Text: Paragraph 23(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall, in the case of a spirits licence, a tobacco licence, a cannabis licence or a vaping product licence, require security in a form satisfactory to the Minister and in an amount determined in accordance with the regulations; and

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 158.34:

Subsection.Text: No person shall, other than in accordance with a vaping product licence issued to the person, manufacture vaping products.

A person that, whether for consideration or otherwise, provides or offers to provide in their place of business equipment for use in that place by another person in the manufacturing of a vaping product is deemed to be manufacturing the vaping product and the other person is deemed not to be manufacturing the vaping product.
An individual who is not a vaping product licensee may manufacture vaping products for their personal use.
Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of a prescribed person that manufactures prescribed vaping products in prescribed circumstances or for a prescribed purpose.

On application in the prescribed form and manner, the Minister may issue, to a vaping product licensee or to a prescribed person that is importing vaping products, stamps the purpose of which is to indicate that vaping duty and, if applicable, additional vaping duty have been paid on a vaping product.
The Minister may limit the quantity of vaping excise stamps that may be issued to a person under subsection (1).
No person shall be issued a vaping excise stamp unless the person has provided security in a form satisfactory to the Minister and in an amount determined in accordance with the regulations.
The Minister may authorize a producer of vaping excise stamps to supply, on the direction of the Minister, vaping excise stamps to a person to which those stamps are issued under subsection (1).
The design and construction of vaping excise stamps shall be subject to the approval of the Minister.

Section.Text: No person shall produce, possess, sell or otherwise supply, or offer to supply, without lawful justification or excuse the proof of which lies on the person, anything that is intended to resemble or pass for a vaping excise stamp.

Section.MarginalNote: Unlawful possession of vaping excise stamps

Subsection.Text: No person shall possess a vaping excise stamp that has not been affixed to the container of a vaping product in the manner prescribed for the purposes of the definition stamped in section 2 to indicate that duty has been paid on the vaping product.
Subsection (1) does not apply to the possession of a vaping excise stamp by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person that lawfully produced the vaping excise stamp;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person to which the vaping excise stamp is issued;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a sufferance warehouse licensee that possesses the vaping excise stamp in their sufferance warehouse on behalf of the person described under paragraph (b); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed person.

Section.MarginalNote: Unlawful supply of vaping excise stamps
Section.Text: No person shall dispose of, sell or otherwise supply, or offer to supply, a vaping excise stamp otherwise than in accordance with this Act.
The Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: cancel a vaping excise stamp that has been issued; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: direct that it be returned or destroyed in a manner specified by the Minister.

Section.Text: No person shall package or stamp a vaping product unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is a vaping product licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person is the importer or owner of the vaping product and the vaping product has been placed in a sufferance warehouse for the purpose of being stamped; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the person is a prescribed person.

Subsection.Text: Except as permitted under section 158.52 or if prescribed circumstances exist, no person shall remove a vaping product from the premises of a vaping product licensee unless it is packaged and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the vaping product is intended for the duty-paid market,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: it is stamped to indicate that vaping duty has been paid, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if additional vaping duty in respect of a specified vaping province is imposed on the vaping product, it is stamped to indicate that the additional vaping duty has been paid; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the vaping product is not intended for the duty-paid market, all vaping product markings that are required under this Act to be printed on, or affixed to, its container are so printed or affixed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to a vaping product licensee that removes from their premises a vaping product if it is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: being removed for

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: delivery to another vaping product licensee,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: export, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: delivery to a person for analysis or destruction in accordance with subparagraph 158.66(a)(iv); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a vaping product drug.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to the removal of a vaping product for analysis or destruction by the Minister.

Section.Text: No person shall purchase or receive for sale a vaping product

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: from a manufacturer that the person knows, or ought to know, is not a vaping product licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is required under this Act to be packaged and stamped unless it is packaged and stamped in accordance with this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that the person knows, or ought to know, is fraudulently stamped.

Section.MarginalNote: Unlawful possession or sale of vaping products

Subsection.Text: Except if prescribed circumstances exist, no person, other than a vaping product licensee, shall dispose of, sell, offer for sale, purchase or have in their possession a vaping product unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is packaged; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is stamped to indicate that vaping duty has been paid.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Unlawful possession or sale — specified vaping province
Subsection.Text: Except if prescribed circumstances exist, no person, other than a vaping product licensee, shall dispose of, sell, offer for sale, purchase or have in their possession a vaping product in a specified vaping province unless it is stamped to indicate that additional vaping duty in respect of the specified vaping province has been paid.
Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply to the possession of vaping products

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of imported vaping products,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: by an excise warehouse licensee in their excise warehouse,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: by a sufferance warehouse licensee in their sufferance warehouse, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: by a customs bonded warehouse licensee in their customs bonded warehouse;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: by a prescribed person that is transporting the vaping products under prescribed circumstances and conditions;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: by a person that possesses the vaping products for analysis or destruction in accordance with subparagraph 158.66(a)(iv);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: by an accredited representative for their personal or official use;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: by an individual who has imported the vaping products for their personal use in quantities not in excess of prescribed limits;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: by an individual who has manufactured the vaping products in accordance with subsection 158.35(3); or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: if the vaping products are vaping product drugs.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception — sale or offer for sale
Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply to the disposal, sale, offering for sale or purchase of a vaping product if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the vaping product is an imported vaping product, an excise warehouse licensee or a customs bonded warehouse licensee sells or offers to sell the vaping product for export and the vaping product is exported by the licensee in accordance with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the vaping product is an imported vaping product and an excise warehouse licensee or a customs bonded warehouse licensee sells or offers to sell the vaping product to an accredited representative for their personal or official use; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the vaping product is a vaping product drug.

Subsection.Text: Except if prescribed circumstances exist, no vaping product licensee shall distribute a vaping product or sell or offer for sale a vaping product to a person unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is packaged;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is stamped to indicate that vaping duty has been paid; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if additional vaping duty in respect of a specified vaping province is imposed on the vaping product, it is stamped to indicate that the additional vaping duty has been paid.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to the distribution, sale or offering for sale of a vaping product by a vaping product licensee

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the distribution, sale or offering for sale is to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: another vaping product licensee, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an accredited representative for their personal or official use;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the vaping product is exported by the vaping product licensee in accordance with this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the vaping product is a vaping product drug.

Section.MarginalNote: Packaging and stamping of vaping products
Section.Text: A vaping product licensee that manufactures a vaping product shall not enter the vaping product into the duty-paid market unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the vaping product has been packaged by the licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the package has printed on it prescribed information;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the vaping product is stamped at the time of packaging to indicate that vaping duty has been paid; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the vaping product is to be entered in the duty-paid market of a specified vaping province, the vaping product is stamped at the time of packaging to indicate that additional vaping duty in respect of the specified vaping province has been paid.

Section.MarginalNote: Packaging and stamping of imported vaping products

Subsection.Text: Except if prescribed circumstances exist, if a vaping product is imported, it must, before it is released under the Customs Act for entry into the duty-paid market,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: be packaged in a package that has printed on it prescribed information;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: be stamped to indicate that vaping duty has been paid; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the vaping product is to be entered in the duty-paid market of a specified vaping province, be stamped to indicate that additional vaping duty in respect of the specified vaping province has been paid.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to a vaping product

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is imported by a vaping product licensee for further manufacturing by the licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that a vaping product licensee is authorized to import under subsection 158.53(2); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that is imported by an individual for their personal use in quantities not in excess of prescribed limits.

Subsection.Text: The absence on a vaping product of stamping that indicates that vaping duty has been paid is notice to all persons that vaping duty has not been paid on the vaping product.

The absence on a vaping product of stamping that indicates that additional vaping duty in respect of a specified vaping province has been paid is notice to all persons that additional vaping duty in respect of the specified vaping province has not been paid on the vaping product.

Section.Text: If vaping products manufactured in Canada are not stamped by a vaping product licensee, the vaping product licensee must immediately enter the vaping products into the licensee’s excise warehouse.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (4), no person shall enter into an excise warehouse a container of vaping products unless the container has printed on it, or affixed to it, vaping product markings and other prescribed information.

Subject to subsections (3) and (4), no person shall deliver a container of imported vaping products that does not have printed on it, or affixed to it, vaping product markings and other prescribed information to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an accredited representative; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a customs bonded warehouse.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Delivery of imported stamped vaping products
Subsection.Text: A container of imported vaping products that were manufactured outside Canada and are stamped may be delivered to a customs bonded warehouse.
A container of vaping products does not require vaping product markings to be printed on it, or affixed to it, if prescribed circumstances exist.

If an imported vaping product intended for the duty-paid market is not stamped to indicate that vaping duty has been paid when it is reported under the Customs Act, it shall be placed in a sufferance warehouse for the purpose of being so stamped.

If an imported vaping product intended for the duty-paid market of a specified vaping province is not stamped to indicate that additional vaping duty in respect of the province has been paid when it is reported under the Customs Act, it shall be placed in a sufferance warehouse for the purpose of being so stamped.
Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply in prescribed circumstances.
No person shall remove a vaping product that is waste from the premises of a vaping product licensee other than the licensee or a person authorized by the Minister.
If a vaping product that is waste is removed from the premises of a vaping product licensee, it shall be dealt with in the manner authorized by the Minister.

Section.MarginalNote: Re-working or destruction of vaping products

Subsection.Text: A vaping product licensee may re-work or destroy a vaping product in the manner authorized by the Minister.
The Minister may authorize a vaping product licensee to import vaping products manufactured in Canada by the licensee for re-working or destruction by the licensee in accordance with subsection (1).

Section.MarginalNote: Responsibility — vaping products manufactured in Canada

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 158.55, a person is responsible for a vaping product manufactured in Canada at any time if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the vaping product licensee that owns the vaping product at that time, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the vaping product is not owned at that time by a vaping product licensee, the vaping product licensee that last owned it; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person is a prescribed person.

Subsection.Text: Subject to sections 158.55 and 158.56, a person is responsible for an imported vaping product at any time if the person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: imported the vaping product; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is a prescribed person.

Section.Text: A person that is responsible for a vaping product ceases to be responsible for it if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is packaged and stamped and the duty on it is paid;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is consumed or used in the manufacturing of a vaping product that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a vaping product drug, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a prescribed vaping product;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is taken for use and the duty on it is paid;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: it is taken for use in accordance with any of subparagraphs 158.66(a)(i) to (iv);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: it is exported;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: it is delivered to an accredited representative for their personal or official use;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: it is lost in prescribed circumstances and the person fulfils any prescribed conditions; or

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: prescribed circumstances exist.

Section.Text: An individual that imports vaping products for their personal use in quantities not in excess of prescribed limits is not responsible for those vaping products.

Heading.TitleText: Imposition and Payment of Duty on Vaping Products
