Section.Text: Duty is imposed on vaping products manufactured in Canada or imported in the amount determined under Schedule 8 and is payable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of vaping products manufactured in Canada, by the vaping product licensee that packaged the vaping products and at the time they are packaged; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of imported vaping products, by the importer, owner or other person that is liable under the Customs Act to pay duty levied under section 20 of the Customs Tariff or that would be liable to pay that duty on the vaping products if they were subject to that duty.

Section.Text: In addition to the duty imposed under section 158.57, a duty in respect of a specified vaping province is imposed on vaping products manufactured in Canada, or imported, in prescribed circumstances in the amount determined in a prescribed manner and is payable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of vaping products manufactured in Canada, by the vaping product licensee that packaged the vaping products and at the time they are packaged; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of imported vaping products, by the importer, owner or other person that is liable under the Customs Act to pay duty levied under section 20 of the Customs Tariff or that would be liable to pay that duty on the vaping products if they were subject to that duty.

Section.Text: The duties imposed under sections 158.57 and 158.58 on imported vaping products shall be paid and collected under the Customs Act, and interest and penalties shall be imposed, calculated, paid and collected under that Act, as if the duties were a duty levied under section 20 of the Customs Tariff, and, for those purposes, the Customs Act applies with any modifications that the circumstances require.

Section.MarginalNote: Duty on vaping products taken for use

Subsection.Text: If a particular person is responsible for vaping products at a particular time when the vaping products are taken for use, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the vaping products are packaged, they are relieved of the duty imposed under section 158.57; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: duty is imposed on the vaping products in the amount determined in respect of the vaping products under Schedule 8.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Specified vaping province — taken for use
Subsection.Text: If a particular person is responsible for vaping products at a particular time when the vaping products are taken for use, a duty in respect of a specified vaping province is imposed on the vaping products in prescribed circumstances in the amount determined in prescribed manner. That duty is in addition to the duty imposed under subsection (1).
The duty imposed under subsection (1) or (2) is payable at the particular time, and by the particular person, referred to in that subsection.

If a particular person that is responsible at a particular time for vaping products cannot account for the vaping products as being, at the particular time, in the possession of a vaping product licensee or in the possession of another person in accordance with subsection 158.44(3), the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the vaping products are packaged, they are relieved of the duty imposed under section 158.57; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: duty is imposed on the vaping products in the amount determined in respect of the vaping products under Schedule 8.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Specified vaping province — unaccounted vaping products
Subsection.Text: If a particular person that is responsible at a particular time for vaping products cannot account for the vaping products as being, at the particular time, in the possession of a vaping product licensee or in the possession of another person in accordance with subsection 158.44(3), a duty in respect of a specified vaping province is imposed on the vaping products in prescribed circumstances in the amount determined in prescribed manner. That duty is in addition to the duty imposed under subsection (1).
The duty imposed under subsection (1) or (2) is payable at the particular time, and by the particular person, referred to in that subsection.
Subsection (1) does not apply in circumstances in which the particular person referred to in that subsection is convicted of an offence under section 218.2.
The duties imposed under sections 158.57 and 158.58 are relieved on a vaping product that is not stamped.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Vaping products imported for personal use
Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to the importation of vaping products by an individual for their personal use to the extent that the quantity of the products imported exceeds the quantity permitted under Chapter 98 of the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff to be imported without the payment of duties, as defined in Note 4 to that Chapter.

Section.MarginalNote: Duty relieved — importation by an individual

Subsection.Text: The duties imposed under sections 158.57 and 158.58 are relieved on vaping products imported by an individual for their personal use if they were manufactured in Canada and are stamped.

The duties imposed under sections 158.57 and 158.58 are relieved on vaping products imported by an individual for their personal use if they were manufactured outside Canada, were previously imported into Canada and are stamped.

Section.Text: The duties imposed under paragraphs 158.57(b) and 158.58(b) are relieved on a stamped vaping product that was manufactured in Canada by a vaping product licensee and that is imported for re-working or destruction in accordance with section 158.53.
The duties imposed under section 158.57 or 158.58 are relieved on a vaping product in prescribed circumstances.
Duty is not payable on a vaping product

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: taken for analysis, or destroyed, by the Minister,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: taken for analysis by a vaping product licensee in a manner approved by the Minister,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: destroyed by a vaping product licensee in a manner approved by the Minister,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: delivered by a vaping product licensee to another person for analysis or destruction by that person in a manner approved by the Minister,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a vaping product drug, or

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a prescribed vaping product; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in prescribed circumstances.

Section.Text: No person shall enter into an excise warehouse

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a vaping product that is stamped; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other vaping product except in accordance with this Act.

Subsection.Text: Except if prescribed circumstances exist, no person shall remove from an excise warehouse vaping products manufactured in Canada.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Removal of Canadian manufactured vaping products
Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, a vaping product manufactured in Canada may be removed from the excise warehouse of the vaping product licensee that manufactured it only if it is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for export by the licensee in accordance with this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery to an accredited representative for their official or personal use.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Removal from warehouse for re-working or destruction
Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, vaping products manufactured in Canada may be removed from the excise warehouse of the vaping product licensee that manufactured them if they are removed for re-working or destruction by the licensee in accordance with section 158.53.
Except if prescribed circumstances exist, no person shall remove imported vaping products from an excise warehouse.
Subject to the regulations, imported vaping products may be removed from an excise warehouse

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery to another excise warehouse;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery to an accredited representative for their official or personal use; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for export by the excise warehouse licensee in accordance with this Act.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 159(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The fiscal months of a person other than a cannabis licensee or a vaping product licensee shall be determined in accordance with the following rules:
Subsection 159(1.01) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Fiscal months — cannabis or vaping product licensee
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, the fiscal months of a cannabis licensee or a vaping product licensee are calendar months.

Section.Text: Section 180 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to this Act, the duty paid on any tobacco product, cannabis product, vaping product or alcohol entered into the duty-paid market shall not be refunded on the exportation of the tobacco product, cannabis product, vaping product or alcohol.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 187.1:

Section.MarginalNote: Refund of duty — destroyed vaping product
Section.Text: The Minister may refund to a vaping product licensee the duty paid on a vaping product that is re-worked or destroyed by the licensee in accordance with section 158.53 if the licensee applies for the refund within two years after the vaping product is re-worked or destroyed.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 206(1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: every person that transports a tobacco product, cannabis product or vaping product that is not stamped or non-duty-paid packaged alcohol.

Subsection.Text: Section 206 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2.01):

Every vaping product licensee shall keep records that will enable the determination of the amount of vaping product manufactured, received, used, packaged, re-worked, sold or disposed of by the licensee.
The portion of section 214 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Every person that contravenes any of sections 25, 25.2 to 25.4, 27 and 29, subsection 32.1(1) and sections 60, 62, 158.02, 158.04 to 158.06, 158.08, 158.1 and 158.37 to 158.39 is guilty of an offence and liable

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 214 of the Act before paragraph (a), as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Every person that contravenes any of sections 25, 25.2 to 25.4, 27 and 29, subsection 32.1(1) and sections 60, 62, 158.02, 158.04 to 158.06, 158.08, 158.1, 158.35, 158.37 to 158.39, 158.41 and 158.43 is guilty of an offence and liable
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 218.1:

Subsection.Text: Every person that contravenes section 158.44 or 158.45 is guilty of an offence and liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine of not less than the amount determined under subsection (2) and not more than the amount determined under subsection (3) or to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine of not less than the amount determined under subsection (2) and not more than the lesser of $500,000 and the amount determined under subsection (3) or to imprisonment for a term of not more than 18 months, or to both.

Subsection.Text: The amount determined under this subsection for an offence under subsection (1) is the greater of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined under Schedule 8 in respect of the vaping products to which the offence relates, using the rates of duty applicable at the time the offence was committed, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the offence occurred in a specified vaping province, the amount determined for A, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 0, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $1,000 in the case of an indictable offence and $500 in the case of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Subsection.Text: The amount determined under this subsection for an offence under subsection (1) is the greater of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined under Schedule 8 in respect of the vaping products to which the offence relates, using the rates of duty applicable at the time the offence was committed, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the offence occurred in a specified vaping province, the amount determined for A, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 0, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $2,000 in the case of an indictable offence and $1,000 in the case of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Section.Text: Paragraph 230(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the commission of an offence under section 214 or subsection 216(1), 218(1), 218.1(1), 218.2(1) or 231(1); or

Section.Text: Paragraph 231(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the commission of an offence under section 214 or subsection 216(1), 218(1), 218.1(1) or 218.2(1); or

Section.Text: Subsection 232(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Part XII.2 of Criminal Code applicable

Subsection.Text: Sections 462.3 and 462.32 to 462.5 of the Criminal Code apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, in respect of proceedings for an offence under section 214, subsection 216(1), 218(1), 218.1(1) or 218.2(1) or section 230 or 231.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 233.1:
Every vaping product licensee that contravenes section 158.46 or 158.49 is liable to a penalty equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined under Schedule 8 in respect of the vaping products to which the contravention relates, using the rates of duty applicable at the time the contravention occurred; and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the contravention occurred in a specified vaping province, the amount determined for A, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 0.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 234(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Every person that contravenes section 38, 40, 49, 61, 62.1, 99, 149, 151, 158.15, 158.5 or 158.67 is liable to a penalty of not more than $25,000.
Subsection 234(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:
Every person that contravenes section 38, 40, 49, 61, 62.1, 99, 149, 151, 158.15, 158.5, 158.52 or 158.67 is liable to a penalty of not more than $25,000.
Section 234 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
Every person that fails to return or destroy stamps as directed by the Minister under paragraph 158.4(b) is liable to a penalty of not more than $25,000.
Subsection 234(4) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (3), is replaced by the following:

Every person that fails to return or destroy stamps as directed by the Minister under paragraph 158.4(b), or that fails to re-work or destroy a vaping product in the manner authorized by the Minister under section 158.53, is liable to a penalty of not more than $25,000.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 234.1:

Every person that contravenes section 158.35, that receives for sale vaping products in contravention of section 158.43 or that sells or offers to sell vaping products in contravention of section 158.44 or 158.45 is liable to a penalty equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined under Schedule 8 in respect of the vaping products to which the contravention relates, using the rates of duty applicable at the time the contravention occurred; and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the contravention occurred in a specified vaping province, the amount determined for A, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 0.

Section.Text: Subsection 237(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every vaping product licensee is liable to a penalty in respect of a vaping product manufactured in Canada that is removed from the excise warehouse of the licensee for a purpose described in subsection 158.68(2) if the product is not delivered or exported, as the case may be, for that purpose.

Every excise warehouse licensee is liable to a penalty in respect of an imported vaping product that is removed from the excise warehouse of the licensee for a purpose described in subsection 158.69(2) if the product is not delivered or exported, as the case may be, for that purpose.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amount of penalty for diversion of vaping products
Subsection.Text: The amount of a penalty for each vaping product that is removed from an excise warehouse for a purpose referred to in subsection (5.1) or (5.2) and that is not delivered or exported, as the case may be, for that purpose is equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined under Schedule 8 in respect of the vaping product, using the rates of duty applicable at the time the vaping product is removed from the excise warehouse; and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if at least one province is prescribed for the purposes of the definition specified vaping province in section 2 at the time the vaping product is removed from the excise warehouse, the amount determined for A, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 0.

Subsection.Text: A licensee that would otherwise be liable to a penalty under this section is not liable if the licensee proves to the satisfaction of the Minister that the alcohol, tobacco product or vaping product that was removed from their excise warehouse or special excise warehouse was returned to that warehouse.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 238:

Section.MarginalNote: Penalty in respect of unaccounted vaping products

Subsection.Text: Every excise warehouse licensee is liable to a penalty in respect of a vaping product entered into their excise warehouse if the licensee cannot account for the vaping product

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as being in the warehouse;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: as having been removed from the warehouse in accordance with this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: as having been destroyed by fire while kept in the warehouse.

Subsection.Text: The amount of a penalty for each vaping product that cannot be accounted for is equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined under Schedule 8 in respect of the vaping product, using the rates of duty applicable at the time the vaping product is entered into the excise warehouse; and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if at least one province is prescribed for the purposes of the definition specified vaping province in section 2 at the time the vaping product is entered into the excise warehouse, the amount determined for A, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 0.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 238.1(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person can demonstrate that the stamps were affixed to tobacco products, cannabis products, vaping products or their containers in the manner prescribed for the purposes of the definition stamped in section 2 and that duty, other than special duty, has been paid on the tobacco products, cannabis products or vaping products; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 238.1(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a vaping excise stamp

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the stamp is in respect of a specified vaping province, $10.00, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, $5.00.

Section.Text: The portion of section 239 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Unless section 237 applies, every person is liable to a penalty equal to 200% of the duty that was imposed on packaged alcohol, a tobacco product, a cannabis product or a vaping product if
Section 264 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Certain things not to be returned
Section.Text: Despite any other provision of this Act, any alcohol, specially denatured alcohol, restricted formulation, raw leaf tobacco, excise stamp, tobacco product, cannabis product or vaping product that is seized under section 260 must not be returned to the person from whom it was seized or any other person unless it was seized in error.
Subsection 266(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (d), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a seized vaping product only to a vaping product licensee.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 304(1)(c.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the types of security that are acceptable for the purposes of subsection 158.03(3) or 158.36(3), and the manner by which the amount of the security is to be determined;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 304(1)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the information to be provided on tobacco products, packaged alcohol, cannabis products and vaping products and on containers of tobacco products, packaged alcohol, cannabis products and vaping products;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 304(1)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the entry and removal of tobacco products, alcohol or vaping products from an excise warehouse or a special excise warehouse;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 304(1)(n) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the sale under section 266 of alcohol, tobacco products, raw leaf tobacco, specially denatured alcohol, restricted formulations, cannabis products or vaping products seized under section 260;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 304.2:

Section.MarginalNote: Definition of coordinated vaping duty system

Subsection.Text: In this section, coordinated vaping duty system means the system providing for the payment, collection and remittance of duty imposed under any of section 158.58 and subsections 158.6(2) and 158.61(2) and any provisions relating to duty imposed under those provisions or to refunds in respect of any such duty.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Coordinated vaping duty system regulations — transition
Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations, in relation to the joining of a province to the coordinated vaping duty system,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing transitional measures, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a tax on the inventory of vaping products held by a vaping product licensee or any other person, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a duty or tax on vaping products that are delivered prior to the province joining that system; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: generally to effect the implementation of that system in relation to the province.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Coordinated vaping duty system regulations — rate variation
Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing rules in respect of whether, how and when a change in the rate of duty for a specified vaping province applies (in this section any such change in the rate of duty is referred to as a “rate variation”), including rules deeming, in specified circumstances and for specified purposes, the status of anything to be different than what it would otherwise be, including when duty is imposed or payable and when duty is required to be reported and accounted for;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if a manner of determining an amount of duty is to be prescribed in relation to the coordinated vaping duty system,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: specifying the circumstances or conditions under which a change in the manner applies, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: prescribing transitional measures in respect of a change in the manner, including

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a tax on the inventory of vaping products held by a vaping product licensee or any other person, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a duty or tax on vaping products that are delivered prior to the change; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing amounts and rates to be used to determine any refund that relates to, or is affected by, the coordinated vaping duty system, excluding amounts that would otherwise be included in determining any such refund, and specifying circumstances under which any such refund shall not be paid or made.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Coordinated vaping duty system regulations — general
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of facilitating the implementation, application, administration and enforcement of the coordinated vaping duty system or a rate variation or the joining of a province to the coordinated vaping duty system, the Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing rules in respect of whether, how and when that system applies and rules in respect of other aspects relating to the application of that system in relation to a specified vaping province, including rules deeming, in specified circumstances and for specified purposes, the status of anything to be different than what it would otherwise be, including when duty is imposed or payable and when duty is required to be reported and accounted for;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing rules related to the movement of vaping products between provinces, including a duty, tax or refund in respect of such movement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: providing for refunds relating to the application of that system in relation to a specified vaping province;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: adapting any provision of this Act or of the regulations made under this Act to the coordinated vaping duty system or modifying any provision of this Act or those regulations to adapt it to the coordinated vaping duty system;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: defining, for the purposes of this Act or the regulations made under this Act, or any provision of this Act or those regulations, in its application to the coordinated vaping duty system, words or expressions used in this Act or those regulations including words or expressions defined in a provision of this Act or those regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: providing that a provision of this Act or of the regulations made under this Act, or a part of such a provision, does not apply to the coordinated vaping duty system;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing compliance measures, including penalties and anti-avoidance rules; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: generally in respect of the application of that system in relation to a province.

Subsection.Text: If a regulation made under this Act in respect of the coordinated vaping duty system states that it applies despite any provision of this Act, in the event of a conflict between the regulation and this Act, the regulation prevails to the extent of the conflict.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding, after Schedule 7, the Schedule 8 set out in Schedule 1 to this Act.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (g)(i) of the definition offence in section 183 of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: section 214 (unlawful production, sale, etc., of tobacco, alcohol, cannabis or vaping products),

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (g) of the definition offence in section 183 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (iii.1):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii.2)
Subparagraph.Text: section 218.2 (unlawful possession, sale, etc., of unstamped vaping products),

Section.Text: The definition excisable goods in subsection 123(1) of the Excise Tax Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: excisable goods means beer or malt liquor (within the meaning assigned by section 4 of the Excise Act) and spirits, wine, tobacco products, cannabis products and vaping products (within the meaning assigned by section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001); (produit soumis à l’accise)

Section.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: coordinated vaping product taxation agreement means an agreement or arrangement entered into by the Minister on behalf of the Government of Canada under Part III.3, including any amendments or variations to the agreement or arrangement made in accordance with that Part; (accord de coordination de la taxation des produits de vapotage)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 8.82:

Subsection.Text: The Minister, with the approval of the Governor in Council, may on behalf of the Government of Canada enter into an agreement or arrangement with the government of a province respecting the taxation of vaping products and, without restricting the generality of the foregoing, respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the collection, administration and enforcement of taxes on vaping products in respect of the province under a single Act of Parliament;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the provision to the Government of Canada by the government of the province, or to the government of the province by the Government of Canada, of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: information acquired in the administration and enforcement of Acts imposing taxes on vaping products and Acts providing for rebates, refunds or reimbursements of taxes on vaping products, paid or payable, or of amounts paid or payable as or on account of the taxation of vaping products, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: other information related to the regulation of vaping and the distribution of vaping products relevant to the system of taxation of vaping products under a single Act of Parliament;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the accounting for taxes collected in accordance with the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the implementation of and transition to the system of taxation of vaping products contemplated under the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: payments, and the eligibility for payments, by the Government of Canada to the government of the province in respect of the revenues from the system of taxation contemplated under the agreement and to which the province is entitled under the agreement, the time when such payments will be made, and the remittance by the government of the province to the Government of Canada of any overpayments by the Government of Canada or the right of the Government of Canada to set off any overpayments against other amounts payable by the Government of Canada to the government of the province, whether under the agreement or any other agreement or arrangement or any Act of Parliament;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the payment by the Government of Canada and its agents and subservient bodies, and by the government of the province and its agents and subservient bodies, of the taxes on vaping products payable under the system of taxation of vaping products contemplated under the agreement and the accounting for the taxes on vaping products so paid;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the compliance by the Government of Canada and its agents and subservient bodies, and by the government of the province and its agents and subservient bodies, with the Act of Parliament under which the system of taxation of vaping products is administered and regulations made under that Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: other matters that relate to, and that are considered advisable for the purposes of implementing or administering, the system of taxation of vaping products contemplated under the agreement.

Subsection.Text: The Minister, with the approval of the Governor in Council, may on behalf of the Government of Canada enter into an agreement with the government of a province amending or varying an agreement or arrangement with the province entered into under subsection (1) or this subsection.

Section.Text: If there is a coordinated vaping product taxation agreement with the government of a province, the appropriate minister may pay to the province out of amounts received in a fiscal year under the Act of Parliament referred to in paragraph 8.9(1)(a)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: amounts determined in accordance with the agreement as provided, and at such times as are specified, in the agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subject to the regulations, advances in respect of the amounts referred to in paragraph (a).

Section.Text: Despite any other Act, the payments paid under a coordinated vaping product taxation agreement under the authority of section 8.91 may be made without any other or further appropriation or authority.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 40(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the calculation and payment to a province of advances on account of any amount that may become payable to the province under this Act, an administration agreement, a reciprocal taxation agreement, a sales tax harmonization agreement, a coordinated cannabis taxation agreement or a coordinated vaping product taxation agreement and the adjustment, by way of reduction or set off, of other payments to the province because of those advances;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 40(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the time and manner of making any payment under this Act, an administration agreement, a sales tax harmonization agreement, a coordinated cannabis taxation agreement or a coordinated vaping product taxation agreement;

Section.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: immediate container has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001; (contenant immédiat)
vaping device has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001; (dispositif de vapotage)
vaping product has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001; (produit de vapotage)
vaping product licensee has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001; (titulaire de licence de produits de vapotage)
vaping substance has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001; (substance de vapotage)

Section.Text: Subsection 97.25(3) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: if the good is a vaping product, to a vaping product licensee;

Section.Text: Subsection 109.2(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Contravention relating to tobacco, cannabis and vaping products and to designated goods
Subsection.Text: Every person that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: removes tobacco products, cannabis products, vaping products or designated goods or causes tobacco products, cannabis products, vaping products or designated goods to be removed from a customs office, sufferance warehouse, bonded warehouse or duty free shop in contravention of this Act or the Customs Tariff or the regulations made under those Acts, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sells or uses tobacco products or designated goods designated as ships’ stores in contravention of this Act or the Customs Tariff or the regulations made under those Acts,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: is liable to a penalty equal to double the total of the duties that would be payable on like tobacco products, cannabis products, vaping products or designated goods released in like condition at the rates of duties applicable to like tobacco products, cannabis products, vaping products or designated goods at the time the penalty is assessed, or to such lesser amount as the Minister may direct.

Section.Text: Subsection 117(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), if spirits, wine, specially denatured alcohol, restricted formulations, cannabis, raw leaf tobacco, excise stamps, tobacco products or vaping products are seized under this Act, they shall not be returned to the person from whom they were seized or any other person unless they were seized in error.

Section.Text: Subsection 119.1(1.1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: a vaping product may only be to a vaping product licensee; and

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 142(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Disposal of things abandoned or forfeit

Subsection.Text: Unless the thing is spirits, specially denatured alcohol, a restricted formulation, wine, raw leaf tobacco, an excise stamp, a tobacco product or a vaping product, anything that has been abandoned to Her Majesty in right of Canada under this Act and anything the forfeiture of which is final under this Act shall
Subsection 142.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Dealing with abandoned or forfeited alcohol, etc.

Subsection.Text: If spirits, specially denatured alcohol, a restricted formulation, wine, raw leaf tobacco, a tobacco product or a vaping product is abandoned or finally forfeited under this Act, the Minister may sell, destroy or otherwise deal with it.
Subsection 142.1(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: a vaping product may only be to a vaping product licensee; and

Section.Text: Paragraph 164(1)(h.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the sale of alcohol, a tobacco product, raw leaf tobacco, specially denatured alcohol, a restricted formulation or a vaping product detained, seized, abandoned or forfeited under this Act;

Section.Text: Paragraph 83(a) of the Customs Tariff is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of goods that would have been classified under tariff item No. 9804.10.00 or 9804.20.00, the value for duty of the goods shall be reduced by an amount equal to that maximum specified value and, in the case of alcoholic beverages, vaping products and tobacco, the quantity of those goods shall, for the purposes of assessing duties other than a duty under section 54 of the Excise Act, 2001, be reduced by the quantity of alcoholic beverages, vaping products and tobacco and up to the maximum quantities specified in tariff item No. 9804.10.00 or 9804.20.00, as the case may be;

Section.Text: Subsection 89(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Relief of the duties or taxes levied or imposed under sections 21.1 to 21.3, the Excise Act, 2001 or the Excise Tax Act may not be granted under subsection (1) on tobacco products, vaping products or designated goods.

Section.Text: Subsection 113(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No refund or drawback of the duties imposed on tobacco products or vaping products under the Excise Act, 2001 shall be granted under subsection (1), except if a refund of the whole or the portion of the duties is required to be granted under Division 3.

The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9804.10.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference beginning with “For the purpose of this tariff item,” and ending with “of manufactured tobacco.” with a reference to “For the purpose of this tariff item, goods may include either wine not exceeding 1.5 litres or any alcoholic beverages not exceeding 1.14 litres, tobacco not exceeding fifty cigars, two hundred cigarettes, two hundred tobacco sticks and two hundred grams of manufactured tobacco, and vaping products not exceeding 120 millilitres of vaping substance in liquid form, or 120 grams of vaping substance in solid form, within any combination of not more than twelve vaping devices and immediate containers.”
Paragraphs (a) and (b) of the Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9804.20.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: goods may include either wine not exceeding 1.5 litres or any alcoholic beverages not exceeding 1.14 litres, tobacco not exceeding fifty cigars, two hundred cigarettes, two hundred tobacco sticks and two hundred grams of manufactured tobacco, and vaping products not exceeding 120 millilitres of vaping substance in liquid form, or 120 grams of vaping substance in solid form, within any combination of not more than twelve vaping devices and immediate containers, if included in the baggage accompanying the person at the time of return to Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if goods (other than alcoholic beverages, cigars, cigarettes, tobacco sticks, manufactured tobacco and vaping products) acquired abroad are not included in the baggage accompanying the person, they may be classified under this tariff item if they are reported by the person at time of return to Canada.

Subsection.Text: The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9804.30.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference beginning with “For the purpose of this tariff item,” and ending with “or manufactured tobacco.” with a reference to “For the purpose of this tariff item, goods shall not include those which could otherwise be imported into Canada free of duties, nor alcoholic beverages, cigars, cigarettes, tobacco sticks, manufactured tobacco or vaping products.”

The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9804.40.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference beginning with “For the purpose of this tariff item,” and ending with “or manufactured tobacco.” with a reference to “For the purpose of this tariff item, goods shall not include alcoholic beverages, cigars, cigarettes, tobacco sticks, manufactured tobacco or vaping products.”
Paragraphs (a) and (b) of the Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9805.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the provisions shall apply to either wine not exceeding 1.5 litres or any alcoholic beverages not exceeding 1.14 litres, tobacco not exceeding fifty cigars, two hundred cigarettes, two hundred tobacco sticks and two hundred grams of manufactured tobacco, and vaping products not exceeding 120 millilitres of vaping substance in liquid form, or 120 grams of vaping substance in solid form, within any combination of not more than twelve vaping devices and immediate containers, if they are included in the baggage accompanying the importer, and no relief from payment of duties is being claimed in respect of alcoholic beverages, tobacco or vaping products under another item in this Chapter at the time of importation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if goods (other than alcoholic beverages, cigars, cigarettes, tobacco sticks, manufactured tobacco and vaping products) are not accompanying the person returning from abroad, they may be classified under this item when imported at a later time if they are reported by the person at the time of return to Canada; and

Subsection.Text: The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9807.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (a)(i), by adding “and” at the end of subparagraph (a)(ii), and by adding the following after subparagraph (a)(ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: vaping products not exceeding 120 millilitres of vaping substance in liquid form, or 120 grams of vaping substance in solid form, within any combination of not more than twelve vaping devices and immediate containers;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of the Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9807.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference to “(other than alcoholic beverages, cigars, cigarettes, tobacco sticks and manufactured tobacco)” with a reference to “(other than alcoholic beverages, cigars, cigarettes, tobacco sticks, manufactured tobacco and vaping products)”.

The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9816.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference to “and not being advertising matter, tobacco or alcoholic beverages,” with a reference to “and not being advertising matter, tobacco, alcoholic beverages or vaping products,”.

The Description of Goods of heading No. 98.25 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference to “alcoholic beverages; tobacco; tobacco products;” with a reference to “alcoholic beverages; tobacco; tobacco products; vaping products;”.

The Description of Goods of heading No. 98.26 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference to “alcoholic beverages; tobacco; tobacco products;” with a reference to “alcoholic beverages; tobacco; tobacco products; vaping products;”.

The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9827.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference beginning with “Goods, which may include” and ending with “of manufactured tobacco,” with a reference to “Goods, which may include either wine not exceeding 1.5 litres or any alcoholic beverages not exceeding 1.14 litres, tobacco products not exceeding fifty cigars, two hundred cigarettes, two hundred tobacco sticks and two hundred grams of manufactured tobacco, and vaping products not exceeding 120 millilitres of vaping substance in liquid form, or 120 grams of vaping substance in solid form, within any combination of not more than twelve vaping devices and immediate containers,”.

The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9906.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference to “other than alcoholic beverages and tobacco products,” with a reference to “other than alcoholic beverages, tobacco products and vaping products,”.

Section.Text: Section 3 of the Tariff Item No. 9805.00.00 Exemption Order is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: vaping products owned by and in the possession of the importer;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition goods in section 2 of the Postal Imports Remission Order is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: alcoholic beverages, cannabis products, vaping products, cigars, cigarettes and manufactured tobacco;

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Order is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: vaping product has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001. (produit de vapotage)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition goods in section 2 of the Courier Imports Remission Order is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: alcoholic beverages, cannabis products, vaping products, cigars, cigarettes and manufactured tobacco;

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Order is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: vaping product has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001. (produit de vapotage)

Section.Text: Subsection 15(4) of the Customs Sufferance Warehouses Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection 39.1(1) of the Act, firearms, prohibited ammunition, prohibited devices, prohibited or restricted weapons, tobacco products and vaping products are goods of a prescribed class that are forfeit if they are not removed from a sufferance warehouse within 14 days after the day on which they were reported under section 12 of the Act.

Section.Text: Paragraph 17(a) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: stamping the goods, if the goods consist of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: imported raw leaf tobacco or imported tobacco products that are placed in the sufferance warehouse in accordance with section 39 of the Excise Act, 2001, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: imported vaping products that are placed in the sufferance warehouse in accordance with section 158.51 of the Excise Act, 2001;

Heading.TitleText: Non-residents’ Temporary Importation of Baggage and Conveyances Regulations

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Non-residents’ Temporary Importation of Baggage and Conveyances Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: immediate container has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001; (contenant immédiat)
vaping device has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001; (dispositif de vapotage)
vaping substance has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001; (substance de vapotage)

Section.Text: Subsection 4(1) of the Regulations is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: 120 millilitres of vaping substance in liquid form, or 120 grams of vaping substance in solid form, within any combination of not more than twelve vaping devices and immediate containers; or

Section.Text: Paragraph 2(b) of the Tariff Item No. 9807.00.00 Exemption Order is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: tobacco products and vaping products;

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Customs Bonded Warehouses Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: vaping product has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001; (produit de vapotage)

Section.Text: Section 14 of the Regulations is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (e), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (f) and by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: vaping products that are not stamped.

Section.Text: The Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 16:

No licensee shall receive into or remove from a bonded warehouse imported vaping products unless they are to be removed from the warehouse for sale to a foreign diplomat in Canada or export from Canada.
Section 18 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsections 37(2) and 39.1(2) of the Customs Act, tobacco products, packaged spirits and vaping products are a prescribed class of goods and are forfeit if they have not been removed from the bonded warehouse within five years of the day on which the goods are described in the form prescribed under subsection 19(2) of that Act.

Heading.TitleText: Regulations Respecting Excise Licences and Registrations

Section.Text: Subparagraph 2(2)(b)(i) of the Regulations Respecting Excise Licences and Registrations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: failed to comply with any Act of Parliament, other than the Act, or of the legislature of a province respecting the taxation of or controls on alcohol, tobacco products or vaping products or any regulations made under it, or

Section.Text: Section 4 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:
A licence is valid for the period specified in the licence, which period shall not exceed

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a vaping product licence, three years; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, two years.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 5(1) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of paragraph 23(3)(b) of the Act, the amount of security to be provided by an applicant for a spirits licence, a tobacco licence, a cannabis licence or a vaping product licence is an amount of not less than $5,000 and
Paragraph 5(1)(b) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a tobacco licence, a cannabis licence or a vaping product licence, be sufficient to ensure payment of the amount of duty referred to in paragraph 160(b) of the Act up to a maximum amount of $5 million per licence.

Section.Text: Paragraph 12(1)(e) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: fails to comply with any Act of Parliament, other than the Act, or of the legislature of a province respecting the taxation of or controls on alcohol, tobacco products or vaping products, or any regulations made under it; or

Heading.TitleText: Regulations Respecting the Possession of Tobacco Products or Cannabis Products That Are Not Stamped

Section.Text: The title of the Regulations Respecting the Possession of Tobacco Products or Cannabis Products That Are Not Stamped is replaced by the following:

AmendedText.LongTitle: Regulations Respecting the Possession of Tobacco, Cannabis or Vaping Products That Are Not Stamped

Section.Text: The Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 1.3:
For the purposes of paragraph 158.44(3)(b) of the Excise Act, 2001, a person may possess a vaping product that is not stamped if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is authorized by an officer under section 19 of the Customs Act to transport vaping products that have been reported under section 12 of that Act and is acting in accordance with that authorization; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person has in their possession documentation that provides evidence that the person is transporting the vaping product on behalf of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a vaping product licensee,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an excise warehouse licensee, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an accredited representative.

Heading.TitleText: Stamping and Marking of Tobacco and Cannabis Products Regulations

Section.Text: The title of the Stamping and Marking of Tobacco and Cannabis Products Regulations is replaced by the following:

AmendedText.LongTitle: Stamping and Marking of Tobacco, Cannabis and Vaping Products Regulations

Section.Text: Paragraph 2(c) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a cannabis product or a vaping product is packaged in a prescribed package when it is packaged in the smallest package — including any outer wrapper, package, box or other container — in which it is sold to the consumer.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 4(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsections 25.1(1) and 158.36(1) of the Act, a prescribed person is a person that satisfies the requirements set out in paragraphs 2(2)(a) to (e) of the Regulations Respecting Excise Licences and Registrations.
Section 4 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
For the purposes of paragraph 158.38(2)(d) of the Act, the following persons are prescribed:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person that transports a vaping excise stamp on behalf of a person described in paragraph 158.38(2)(a) or (b) of the Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person that has in their possession vaping excise stamps only for the purpose of applying adhesive to the stamps on behalf of the vaping product licensee to which the stamps are issued.

Section.Text: The Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 4:

Subsection.Text: If the Minister holds, at any time in a calendar month, security that a person has provided under subsection 158.36(3) of the Act and if the person is not a vaping product licensee throughout the calendar month, the person must file with the Minister an information return for the calendar month in respect of the possession and use of any vaping excise stamps issued to the person.
The information return of a person for a particular calendar month must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: be made in prescribed form containing prescribed information; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: be filed in prescribed manner on or before the last day of the first calendar month following the particular calendar month.

Section.Text: The Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 4.1:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) to (4), the amount of security for the purpose of subsection 158.36(3) of the Act is the greater of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $1.00 multiplied by the number of vaping excise stamps that either are in the applicant’s possession at the time of application or are to be issued in respect of the application, and

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (3) and (4), if the amount determined under paragraph (1)(a) is greater than $5 million, the amount of security for the purpose of subsection 158.36(3) of the Act is $5 million.

If a person has provided security under paragraph 23(3)(b) of the Act in an amount that is equal to or greater than the amount of security determined in accordance with subsections (1) and (2), the amount of security for the purpose of subsection 158.36(3) of the Act is nil.

If a person has provided security under paragraph 23(3)(b) of the Act in an amount that is less than the amount of security determined in accordance with subsections (1) and (2), the amount of security for the purpose of subsection 158.36(3) of the Act is the difference between the amount of security determined in accordance with subsections (1) and (2) and the amount of security provided by the person under paragraph 23(3)(b) of the Act.

Section.Text: The portion of section 4.2 of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of the definition stamped in section 2 of the Act and subsections 25.3(1), 158.05(1) and 158.38(1) of the Act, the prescribed manner of affixing an excise stamp to a package is by affixing the stamp
The Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 5:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraphs 158.44(3)(e) and 158.47(2)(c) and section 158.56 of the Act, the prescribed limit is five units of vaping products.

For the purposes of subsection (1), a unit of vaping products consists of 120 millilitres of vaping substance in liquid form, or 120 grams of vaping substance in solid form, within any combination of not more than 12 vaping devices and immediate containers.

Section.Text: The heading after section 7 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection 158.5(1) of the Act, the required vaping product markings are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for containers of vaping products manufactured in Canada, the marking set out in Schedule 7; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for containers of vaping products manufactured outside Canada, the marking set out in Schedule 8.

Subsection.Text: The vaping product markings must be printed on or affixed to the container in a conspicuous manner and in accordance with the specifications set out in the appropriate Schedule.
For the purposes of subsection 158.5(2) of the Act, the required vaping product marking is the marking set out in Schedule 8.
The vaping product marking must be printed on or affixed to the container in a conspicuous manner and in accordance with the specifications set out in Schedule 8.

Section.Text: Schedule 7 to the Regulations is amended by replacing the reference after the heading “SCHEDULE 7” with the following:
The heading of Schedule 7 to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

ScheduleFormHeading.TitleText: Marking for Containers of Manufactured Tobacco, Cigars and Vaping Products Manufactured in Canada

Section.Text: Schedule 8 to the Regulations is amended by replacing the reference after the heading “SCHEDULE 8” with the following:
The heading of Schedule 8 to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

ScheduleFormHeading.TitleText: Marking for Containers of Manufactured Tobacco, Cigars and Vaping Products Manufactured Outside Canada, Containers of Cigars Manufactured in Canada and Intended for Delivery to a Duty Free Shop or as Ships’ Stores and Containers of Imported Manufactured Tobacco and Cigars Referred to in Subsection 38(2) of the Act

Subsection.Text: Sections 158.35, 158.51 to 158.53, 158.68 and 158.69 of the Excise Act, 2001, as enacted by section 59, subsection 64(2), sections 65 to 69, subsections 70(2) and (4), sections 71, 72 and 75, subsection 81(2) and sections 82, 87 to 105, 115 and 122 come into force on October 1, 2022.

Sections 158.41, 158.57 and 158.58 of the Excise Act, 2001, as enacted by section 59, apply in respect of vaping products manufactured in Canada that are packaged on or after October 1, 2022 and to vaping products that are imported into Canada or released (as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act) on or after that day. Those sections of the Excise Act, 2001 also apply in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: vaping products manufactured in Canada that are packaged before October 1, 2022 if the vaping products are stamped after the day on which this Act receives royal assent; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: vaping products that are imported into Canada or released (as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act) after the day on which this Act receives royal assent but before October 1, 2022 if the vaping products are stamped when they are reported under the Customs Act.

Subsection.Text: Sections 158.42 to 158.47 and 158.49, subsection 158.5(2), sections 158.54 to 158.56, 158.6 and 158.61 of the Excise Act, 2001, as enacted by section 59, subsection 63(1) and sections 107 to 109 come into force on October 1, 2022. However, those provisions of the Excise Act, 2001, subsection 63(1) and sections 107 to 109 do not apply before 2023 in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: vaping products manufactured in Canada that are packaged before October 1, 2022 and that are not stamped; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: vaping products that are imported into Canada or released (as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act) before October 1, 2022 and that are not stamped.

Subsection.Text: In applying sections 158.57 and 158.58 of the Excise Act, 2001, as enacted by section 59, in respect of vaping products manufactured in Canada that are packaged before October 1, 2022, paragraph (a) of each of those sections 158.57 and 158.58 is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of vaping products manufactured in Canada, by the vaping product licensee that packaged the vaping products and at the later of the beginning of October 1, 2022 and the time they are stamped; and

Subsection.Text: Section 87 of the Excise Act, 2001 is amended by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (a) and by repealing paragraph (a.1).
Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on June 30, 2022.
Paragraph 88(2)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: that is wine referred to in paragraph 135(2)(b) may be possessed by any person; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on June 30, 2022, but does not apply to wine packaged before that day.
Subsection 134(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection (1) does not apply to wine

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is produced by an individual for their personal use and that is consumed in the course of that use; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is produced in Canada from honey or apples and composed wholly of agricultural or plant product grown in Canada.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies after June 29, 2022.
Paragraph 135(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: produced in Canada from honey or apples and composed wholly of agricultural or plant product grown in Canada;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to wine packaged on or after June 30, 2022.
The portion of the definition beer or malt liquor in section 4 of the Excise Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: beer or malt liquor means any product (other than wine, as defined in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001) containing more than 0.5% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume that is

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on July 1, 2022.
Subsection 170.1(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

In subsection (1), the reference to “first 75,000 hectolitres of beer and malt liquor brewed in Canada” does not include beer or malt liquor that is exported or deemed to be exported under section 173.
Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on July 1, 2022.
The Select Luxury Items Tax Act, whose text is as follows and whose schedule is set out in Schedule 2 to this Act, is enacted:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act respecting the taxation of select luxury items

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Select Luxury Items Tax Act.

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: assessment means an assessment under this Act and includes a reassessment. (cotisation)

bank means a bank as defined in section 2 of the Bank Act or an authorized foreign bank, as defined in that section, that is not subject to the restrictions and requirements referred to in subsection 524(2) of that Act. (banque)
calendar quarter means a period of three months beginning on the first day of January, April, July or October. (trimestre civil)
Commissioner means, except in sections 80 and 81 and subsections 153(1) to (8) and (19), the Commissioner of Revenue appointed under section 25 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act. (commissaire)

common-law partner of an individual at a particular time means a person who is the common-law partner of the individual at the particular time for the purposes of the Income Tax Act. (conjoint de fait)
confirmed delivery service means certified or registered mail or any other service that provides a record that a notice or document has been sent or delivered. (service de messagerie)
consideration includes any amount that is payable by operation of law. (contrepartie)
credit union has the same meaning as in subsection 137(6) of the Income Tax Act. (caisse de crédit)
export means export from Canada. (exportation)
government entity means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a department or agency of the government of Canada or of a province;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a municipality;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an aboriginal government as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation all of the shares (except directors’ qualifying shares) of the capital stock of which are owned by one or more persons each of which is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a municipality, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a corporation described in this paragraph; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a board or commission, established by Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province, that performs an administrative or regulatory function of government, or by a municipality, that performs an administrative or regulatory function of a municipality. (entité gouvernementale)

Definition.Text: guest of a particular person on a subject item means an individual that uses or enjoys the subject item and that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: does not deal at arm’s length with the particular person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is an employee of the particular person or of a person that does not deal at arm’s length with the particular person; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: uses or enjoys the subject item, at the invitation of the particular person or a person referred to in paragraph (a) or (b), for no consideration or for nominal consideration. (invité)

Definition.Text: identification number of a subject item means an identification number that is satisfactory to the Minister and is unique to the subject item. (numéro d’identification)
import means import into Canada. (importation)
Indigenous governing body has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Department of Indigenous Services Act. (corps dirigeant autochtone)
judge, in respect of any matter, means a judge of a superior court having jurisdiction in the province in which the matter arises or a judge of the Federal Court. (juge)

military authority means the Canadian Forces, within the meaning of section 14 of the National Defence Act, the Department of National Defence or a visiting force, as defined in section 2 of the Visiting Forces Act. (autorité militaire)
Minister means the Minister of National Revenue. (ministre)
municipality means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an incorporated city, town, village, metropolitan authority, township, district, county or rural municipality or other incorporated municipal body however designated; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other local authority that the Minister may determine to be a municipality for the purposes of this Act. (municipalité)

Definition.Text: officer means, except in sections 75, 127 and 149,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is appointed or employed in the administration or enforcement of this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to imported goods that have not been released under the Customs Act, an officer as defined in subsection 2(1) of that Act. (préposé)

Definition.Text: passenger seat means a seat on an aircraft other than a pilot seat. (siège passager)

person means an individual, a partnership, a corporation, the estate or succession of a deceased individual, a trust, a joint venture, a government or a body that is a society, a union, a club, an association, a commission or another organization of any kind. (personne)

personal representative, of a deceased individual or the estate or succession of a deceased individual, means the executor of the individual’s will, the liquidator of the individual’s succession, the administrator of the estate or any person that is responsible under the appropriate law for the proper collection, administration, disposition and distribution of the assets of the estate or succession. (représentant personnel)
pilot seat includes a flight engineer seat or a flight deck observer seat. (siège destiné au pilote)
police authority means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Royal Canadian Mounted Police, the Ontario Provincial Police, the Sûreté du Québec, the Canadian Coast Guard or a municipal or regional police force established in accordance with provincial legislation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a government entity that is responsible for the preservation and maintenance of the public peace; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed person. (corps policier)

Definition.Text: prescribed means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a form or the manner of filing a form, authorized by the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of the information to be given on or with a form, specified by the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, prescribed by regulation or determined in accordance with rules prescribed by regulation. (Version anglaise seulement)

Definition.Text: qualifying aircraft user means a person (other than a prescribed person) that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person that is an agent of Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a municipality;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an Indigenous governing body;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a police authority;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a government entity that has as its primary responsibility the conduct of emergency medical response activities or emergency fire response activities;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a government entity that has as its primary responsibility the operation, management and maintenance of a hospital;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a person that has as its primary responsibility the operation, management and maintenance of a listed airport, as defined in section 2 of the Air Travellers Security Charge Act;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: NAV CANADA, a corporation incorporated on May 26, 1995 under Part II of the Canada Corporations Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed person. (utilisateur admissible d’aéronef)

Definition.Text: record means any material on which representations, in any form, of information or concepts are recorded or marked and that is capable of being read or understood by a person, a computer system or other device. (registre)
registered vendor, in respect of a type of subject item, means a person that is registered under Division 5 as a vendor in respect of that type of subject item. (vendeur inscrit)
select subject vessel means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a subject vessel (other than a prescribed subject vessel) that is equipped with a bed, bunk, berth or similar sleeping amenity; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed subject vessel. (navire assujetti désigné)

Definition.Text: subject aircraft means an aircraft that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an aeroplane, glider or helicopter, as those terms are defined in subsection 101.01(1) of the Canadian Aviation Regulations, that has a date of manufacture after 2018 if the aircraft

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is equipped only with one or more pilot seats and cannot have any other seating configuration,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is equipped only with one or more pilot seats, or is not equipped with any seats, and cannot have a seating configuration, excluding pilot seats, of 40 or greater, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: is equipped with one or more pilot seats and one or more passenger seats and has a seating configuration, excluding pilot seats, of 39 or fewer, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed aircraft,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an aircraft that is designed and equipped for military activities,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an aircraft that is equipped for the carriage of goods only,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an aircraft

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that is registered with a government before September 2022 otherwise than solely for a purpose incidental to its manufacture, offering for sale or transportation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which a user of the aircraft has possession before September 2022,

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a subject vehicle, or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed aircraft. (aéronef assujetti)

Definition.Text: subject item means a subject aircraft, a subject vehicle or a subject vessel. (bien assujetti)
subject vehicle means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a motor vehicle that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is designed or adapted primarily to carry individuals on highways and streets,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has a seating capacity of not more than 10 individuals,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: has a gross vehicle weight rating, as that term is defined in subsection 2(1) of the Motor Vehicle Safety Regulations, that is less than or equal to 3,856 kg,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: has a date of manufacture after 2018, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: is designed to travel with four or more wheels in contact with the ground, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed motor vehicle,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an ambulance,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a hearse,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a motor vehicle that is clearly marked for policing activities,

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a motor vehicle that is clearly marked and equipped for emergency medical response activities or emergency fire response activities,

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a recreational vehicle that is designed or adapted to provide temporary residential accommodations, and is equipped with at least four of the following elements:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: cooking facilities,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a refrigerator or ice box,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a self-contained toilet,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a heating or air-conditioning system that can function independently of the vehicle engine,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a potable water supply system that includes a faucet and sink, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a 110-V to 125-V electric power supply, or a liquefied petroleum gas supply, that can function independently of the vehicle engine,

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a motor vehicle

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that is registered before September 2022 with a government, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which possession was transferred to a user of the motor vehicle before September 2022, or

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed motor vehicle. (véhicule assujetti)

Definition.Text: subject vessel means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a vessel that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is designed or adapted for leisure, recreation or sport activities, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has a date of manufacture after 2018, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed vessel,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
