Paragraph.Text: amounts borrowed by the Minister under an order made under paragraph 46.1(a) of that Act for the payment of any amount in respect of a debt that was originally incurred under an order made under paragraph 46.1(c) of that Act, other than in respect of a debt that was originally incurred under such an order made during the period beginning on March 23, 2021 and ending on May 6, 2021.

Section.Text: Section 8 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (2), any amounts borrowed by the Minister during the period beginning on March 23, 2021 and ending on May 6, 2021 under an order made under paragraph 46.1(c) of the Financial Administration Act do not count in the calculation of the amounts referred to in paragraph (1)(b).

Section.Text: Paragraph 49(1)(a.1) of the Financial Administration Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: the money that is borrowed under an order made under paragraph 46.1(c) of the Financial Administration Act, other than that borrowed under such an order made during the period beginning on March 23, 2021 and ending on May 6, 2021, and that is due at the end of the fiscal year to which the Public Accounts relate; and

Section.Text: The Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985 is amended by adding the following after section 9.16:

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, a defined benefit plan, other than a negotiated contribution plan, may provide for the establishment of a solvency reserve account in the plan’s pension fund.
Subject to the regulations, an employer may make payments into the solvency reserve account.
The administrator shall not transfer into the solvency reserve account, nor permit to be transferred into that account, any moneys that are held in the pension fund outside of that account.

Despite any terms of the pension plan or any document that creates or supports the plan or the pension fund, amounts may be withdrawn from the solvency reserve account in accordance with the regulations.
Section 9.2 does not apply with respect to a withdrawal from the solvency reserve account.

Section.Text: Section 10 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):

Subsection.Text: The administrator of a pension plan shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establish, before the plan is filed for registration, a governance policy that contains the prescribed information; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ensure that the policy complies with this Act and the regulations.

Subsection.Text: The administrator is not required to file the governance policy for the purposes of subsection (1) or to file any amendment to the policy for the purposes of subsection 10.1(1).

An administrator of a pension plan that was registered or was filed for registration under this section before the day on which subsection (7) comes into force shall, within one year after that day, establish the governance policy for the plan.
Subsection 39(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h):

Paragraph.Label: (h.01)
Paragraph.Text: respecting solvency reserve accounts;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 39(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (n.1):

Paragraph.Label: (n.11)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the investment of the assets of a pension fund;

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Budget Implementation Act, 2021, No. 1.
If section 188 of the other Act comes into force before section 187 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 187 is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Section 10 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (10):

Subsection.Text: The administrator of a pension plan shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establish, before the plan is filed for registration, a governance policy that contains the prescribed information; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ensure that the policy complies with this Act and the regulations.

Subsection.Text: The administrator is not required to file the governance policy for the purposes of subsection (1) or to file any amendment to the policy for the purposes of subsection 10.1(1).

An administrator of a pension plan, other than a negotiated contribution plan, that was registered or was filed for registration under this section before the day on which subsection (11) comes into force shall, within one year after that day, establish the governance policy for the plan.

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the day on which that section 187 comes into force, subsections 10(7) to (10) of the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985 are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The administrator of a negotiated contribution plan shall, before the plan is filed for registration, establish a funding policy that contains the prescribed information.
The administrator is not required to file the funding policy for the purposes of subsection (1) or to file any amendment to the policy for the purposes of subsection 10.1(1).
The administrator shall ensure that the funding policy complies with this Act and the regulations.

An administrator of a negotiated contribution plan that was registered or was filed for registration under this section before the day on which subsection (7), as enacted by section 188 of the Budget Implementation Act, 2021, No. 1, comes into force shall, within one year after that day, establish the funding policy referred to in subsection (7) and the governance policy referred to in subsection (11).
If section 187 of this Act comes into force before section 188 of the other Act, then that section 188 is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Section 10 of the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985 is amended by adding the following after subsection (9):

Subsection.Text: The administrator of a negotiated contribution plan shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establish, before the plan is filed for registration, a funding policy that contains the prescribed information; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ensure that the policy complies with this Act and the regulations.

Subsection.Text: The administrator is not required to file the funding policy for the purposes of subsection (1) or to file any amendment to the policy for the purposes of subsection 10.1(1).

An administrator of a negotiated contribution plan that was registered or was filed for registration under this section before the day on which subsection (10) comes into force shall establish the funding policy within one year after that day.

If section 188 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 187 of this Act, then that section 187 is deemed to have come into force before that section 188 and subsection (3) applies as a consequence.
Section 186 and subsection 188(1) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Section 187 comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Special Import Measures Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (10):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Assessment of injury — impacts on workers
Subsection.Text: In any assessment of injury under this Act, any impacts on workers employed in the domestic industry shall be taken into account.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Assessment of retardation — impacts on jobs
Subsection.Text: In any assessment of retardation under this Act, any impacts on jobs shall be taken into account.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following before section 3:
In this Part, massive importation includes a series of importations into Canada that in the aggregate are massive and have occurred within a relatively short period of time.
The portion of paragraph 5(a) of the Act after subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: injury has been caused by a massive importation of the goods into Canada and the goods are likely to seriously undermine the remedial effect of the duties applicable under subsection 3(1); and

Section.Text: Subparagraphs 6(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: injury has been caused by a massive importation of the goods into Canada, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the goods are likely to seriously undermine the remedial effect of the duties applicable under subsection 3(1),

Section.Text: Subsection 31(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The period of 30 days referred to in subsection (1) is extended to 45 days if, before the expiry of the 30 days, the President causes written notice to be given to the complainant that the period of 30 days is insufficient to determine whether there is compliance with the conditions referred to in subsection (2) or the condition referred to in subsection 31.1(1).
Paragraphs 32(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the complaint is properly documented, cause the complainant to be informed in writing that the complaint was received and that it is properly documented; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the complaint is not properly documented, cause the complainant to be informed in writing that the complaint was received and indicate the information and material needed in order for the complaint to be properly documented.

Subsection.Text: Section 32 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

If the President receives a properly documented written complaint under subsection (1), the President shall cause the government of the country of export to be notified in writing that the complaint was received and that it is properly documented.
The notice shall be provided no later than

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a complaint respecting the dumping of goods, seven days before the day on which the President decides whether or not to cause an investigation to be initiated; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a complaint respecting the subsidizing of goods, 20 days before the day on which the President decides whether or not to cause an investigation to be initiated.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 42(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The Tribunal, forthwith after receipt of a notice of a preliminary determination under subsection 38(3), shall make inquiry with respect to the following matters:
The portion of paragraph 42(1)(b) of the Act after subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: injury has been caused by a massive importation of the goods into Canada and the goods are likely to seriously undermine the remedial effect of the duties applicable under subsection 3(1); and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 42(1)(c)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: injury has been caused by a massive importation of the goods into Canada, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the goods are likely to seriously undermine the remedial effect of the duties applicable under subsection 3(1).

Section.Text: Paragraph 71(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the change in trade pattern is caused by the imposition of anti-dumping or countervailing duties.

Section.Text: Subsection 72(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The President shall cause an investigation to be initiated respecting the circumvention of an order or finding of the Tribunal, or an order of the Governor in Council imposing a countervailing duty under section 7, on the President’s own initiative or, if he or she receives a written complaint respecting the circumvention, within 45 days after the day on which that complaint is received, if he or she is of the opinion that there is evidence disclosing a reasonable indication that circumvention is occurring.

Section.Text: Subsection 76.01(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An order made on the completion of an interim review, other than an order rescinding an order or finding, expires on the day on which the Tribunal makes an order under subsection 76.03(12).
The portion of subsection 76.03(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The Tribunal shall initiate an expiry review with respect to an order or finding described in any of subsections 3(1) and (2) and sections 4 to 6 before the expiry of five years after whichever of the following days is applicable:
Subsections 76.03(2) to (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The Tribunal may terminate an expiry review at any time if, in the Tribunal’s opinion, the review is not supported by domestic producers. Upon terminating a review, the Tribunal shall without delay cause notice of the termination to be given to the President and all other persons and governments specified in the rules of the Tribunal.
The portion of subsection 76.03(6) of the Act before subparagraph (a)(i) is replaced by the following:
Upon initiating an expiry review, the Tribunal shall without delay

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: cause notice of the review to be given to

Subsection.Text: Subsection 76.03(6) of the Act is amended by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (a) and by repealing paragraph (b).
The portion of subsection 76.03(7) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Unless the expiry review is terminated under subsection (2), the President shall
Paragraph 76.03(12)(a) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (i) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which it terminated an expiry review under subsection (2); or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 76.03(13)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an order made by the Tribunal under section 75.3 or subsection 75.4(8) or 75.6(7) amending the order or finding under review, if that order is made on or after the day on which the review is initiated under subsection (1) but before the day on which the order of the Tribunal is made under subsection (12); and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 76.03(14) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An order made as a result of a decision by the President setting out a finding of circumvention or an interim review decision of the President relating to a finding of circumvention, other than an order rescinding the extension of duties or exempting an exporter from the extension of duties, expires on the day on which the Tribunal makes an order under subsection (12).

Section.Text: Paragraph (g) of the definition definitive decisions in subsection 77.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: an order of the Tribunal under subsection 76.01(4),

Section.Text: Paragraph (g) of the definition definitive decision in subsection 77.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: an order of the Tribunal under subsection 76.01(4),

Section.Text: Section 88.1 of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 96.1(1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an order of the Tribunal under subsection 76.01(4);

Section.Text: Subparagraph 97(1)(a.1)(v) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: whether a change in a pattern of trade is caused by the imposition of an anti-dumping or countervailing duty, and

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section and sections 208 to 211.

Definition.Text: commencement day means the day on which this Act receives royal assent. (date de référence)
former Act means the Special Import Measures Act as it read on the day before the commencement day. (ancienne loi)

Subsection.Text: Words and expressions used in sections 208 to 211 have the same meaning as in the Special Import Measures Act.

Section.Text: If, before the commencement day, the President receives a written complaint respecting the dumping or subsidizing of goods under subsection 31(1) of the former Act, any proceeding, process or action in respect of the complaint is to be continued and disposed of in accordance with that Act.

If, before the commencement day, the President receives a written complaint under subsection 72(1) of the former Act respecting the circumvention of an order or finding of the Tribunal, or an order of the Governor in Council imposing a countervailing duty under section 7 of that Act, any proceeding, process or action in respect of the complaint is to be continued and disposed of in accordance with that Act.

Subsection.Text: If, before the commencement day, the Tribunal receives a request to conduct an interim review of an order or finding, or any aspect of an order or finding, under subsection 76.01(1) of the former Act, any interim review is to be initiated — or, if already initiated, continued — and disposed of in accordance with that Act.

If, before the commencement day, the Tribunal initiates, on its own initiative, an interim review of an order or finding, or any aspect of an order or finding, under subsection 76.01(1) of the former Act, the interim review is to be continued and disposed of in accordance with that Act.

Section.Text: If, before the commencement day, a notice of expiry respecting an order or finding has been published under subsection 76.03(2) of the former Act, any expiry review in respect of the order or finding is to be initiated — or, if already initiated, continued — and disposed of in accordance with that Act.
Subsection 2(1) of the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: trade union means an employee organization that has been certified under federal or provincial law, or recognized by the employer, as a bargaining agent; (syndicat)

Section.Text: Paragraph 16(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: consider complaints and extension requests filed with the Tribunal under this Act by domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods or by trade unions whose members are engaged in the Canadian production of like or directly competitive goods and, if appropriate, conduct inquiries into the complaints and extension requests and report on them;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 23(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Any of the following may file a written complaint with the Tribunal alleging that goods are being imported into Canada in such increased quantities and under such conditions as to cause or threaten serious injury to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a domestic producer of the like or directly competitive goods;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person or association acting on behalf of the domestic producer;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a trade union whose members are engaged in the Canadian production of the like or directly competitive goods.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 23(2)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a complaint filed by or on behalf of a domestic producer, state an estimate of the total percentage of Canadian production of the like or directly competitive goods that is produced by the producer;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a complaint filed by a trade union,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: state an estimate of the total percentage of Canadian production of the like or directly competitive goods that is produced by its members, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: provide evidence that one or more domestic producers of the like or directly competitive goods support the complaint and state an estimate of the total percentage of Canadian production of the like or directly competitive goods that is produced by those producers; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: make any other representations that the complainant considers relevant to the matter.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 23(3)(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: such information as is available to the complainant to prove the facts referred to in paragraph (2)(a) and to substantiate the estimates referred to in paragraph (2)(b) or (b.1); and

Section.Text: Paragraph 26(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the complaint is made by or on behalf of, or with the support of, domestic producers who produce a major proportion of domestic production of the like or directly competitive goods; and

Section.Text: Section 30.04 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: An extension request may also be filed by a trade union whose members are engaged in the Canadian production of the like or directly competitive goods.
Paragraphs 30.05(1)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an extension request filed by or on behalf of a domestic producer, state an estimate of the total percentage of Canadian production of the like or directly competitive goods that is produced by the producer;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an extension request filed by a trade union,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: state an estimate of the total percentage of Canadian production of the like or directly competitive goods that is produced by its members, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: provide evidence that one or more domestic producers of the like or directly competitive goods support the request and state an estimate of the total percentage of Canadian production of the like or directly competitive goods that is produced by those producers; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: make any other representations that the requester considers relevant to the matter.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 30.05(2)(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: such information as is available to the requester to prove the facts referred to in paragraph (1)(a) and to substantiate the estimates referred to in paragraph (1)(b) or (b.1); and

Section.Text: Paragraph 30.07(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the extension request is made by or on behalf of, or with the support of, domestic producers who produce a major proportion of domestic production of the like or directly competitive goods.

Section.Text: Paragraph 39(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: specifying any additional information that must accompany a complaint filed under any of subsections 23(1) to (1.1), 30.01(2), 30.011(1), 30.012(2), 30.11(1), 30.22(1) and 30.23(1) or an extension request filed under subsection 30.04(1) or (1.1) or 30.25(3); and

Section.Text: Subsection 160.04(1) of the Trust and Loan Companies Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and 143(2), the management of a company shall, concurrently with sending notice of a meeting of shareholders, send a form of proxy that is in accordance with the regulations to each shareholder entitled to receive notice of the meeting.
Subsections 160.05(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

A person shall not solicit proxies unless a proxy circular that is in accordance with the regulations is sent to the auditor of the company, to each shareholder whose proxy is solicited and, in the case set out in paragraph (b), to the company as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of solicitation by or on behalf of the management of a company, a management proxy circular, either as an appendix to or as a separate document accompanying the notice of the meeting; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of any other solicitation, a dissident’s proxy circular stating the purposes of the solicitation.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception — solicitation to 15 or fewer shareholders
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), a person may solicit proxies, other than by or on behalf of the management of the company, without sending a dissident’s proxy circular, if the total number of shareholders whose proxies are solicited is 15 or fewer, two or more joint holders being counted as one shareholder.

Despite subsection (1), a person may solicit proxies, other than by or on behalf of the management of the company, without sending a dissident’s proxy circular if the solicitation is, in the prescribed circumstances, conveyed by public broadcast, speech or publication.

A person who sends a management proxy circular or dissident’s proxy circular shall concurrently send to the Superintendent a copy of it together with the form of proxy, any other documents for use in connection with the meeting and, in the case of a management proxy circular, a copy of the notice of meeting.
Subsection 160.05(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Superintendent shall publish in a publication generally available to the public, a notice of a decision made by the Superintendent granting an exemption under subsection (3).

Section.Text: Section 160.071 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the powers that may be granted by a shareholder in a form of proxy;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting proxy circulars and forms of proxy, including the form and content of those documents; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the conditions under which a company is exempt from any of the requirements of sections 160.02 to 160.07.

Section.Text: Subsection 164.03(1) of the Insurance Companies Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and 144(2), the management of a company shall, concurrently with sending notice of a meeting of shareholders and policyholders, send a form of proxy that is in accordance with the regulations to each shareholder entitled to receive notice of the meeting and to each policyholder entitled to receive notice of the meeting under section 143.
Subsections 164.04(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

A person shall not solicit proxies unless a proxy circular that is in accordance with the regulations is sent to the auditor of the company, to each shareholder or policyholder whose proxy is solicited and, in the case set out in paragraph (b), to the company as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of solicitation by or on behalf of the management of a company, a management proxy circular, either as an appendix to or as a separate document accompanying the notice of the meeting; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of any other solicitation, a dissident’s proxy circular stating the purposes of the solicitation.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), a person may solicit proxies, other than by or on behalf of the management of the company, without sending a dissident’s proxy circular, if the total number of shareholders and policyholders whose proxies are solicited is 15 or fewer, with two or more joint holders being counted as one shareholder.

Despite subsection (1), a person may solicit proxies, other than by or on behalf of the management of the company, without sending a dissident’s proxy circular if the solicitation is, in the prescribed circumstances, conveyed by public broadcast, speech or publication.

A person who sends a management proxy circular or dissident’s proxy circular shall concurrently send to the Superintendent a copy of it together with the form of proxy, any other documents for use in connection with the meeting and, in the case of a management proxy circular, a copy of the notice of meeting.
Subsection 164.04(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Superintendent shall publish in a publication generally available to the public, a notice of a decision made by the Superintendent granting an exemption under subsection (3).

Section.Text: Section 164.061 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the powers that may be granted by a shareholder or a policyholder in a form of proxy;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting proxy circulars and forms of proxy, including the form and content of those documents; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the conditions under which a company is exempt from any of the requirements of sections 164.01 to 164.06.

Section.Text: Subsection 788(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and 768(2), the management of an insurance holding company shall, concurrently with sending notice of a meeting of shareholders, send a form of proxy that is in accordance with the regulations to each shareholder entitled to receive notice of the meeting under section 767.
Subsections 789(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

A person shall not solicit proxies unless a proxy circular that is in accordance with the regulations is sent to the auditor of the insurance holding company, to each shareholder whose proxy is solicited and, in the case set out in paragraph (b), to the bank as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of solicitation by or on behalf of the management of an insurance holding company, a management proxy circular, either as an appendix to or as a separate document accompanying the notice of the meeting; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of any other solicitation, a dissident’s proxy circular stating the purposes of the solicitation.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), a person may solicit proxies, other than by or on behalf of the management of the insurance holding company, without sending a dissident’s proxy circular, if the total number of shareholders whose proxies are solicited is 15 or fewer, with two or more joint holders being counted as one shareholder.

Despite subsection (1), a person may solicit proxies, other than by or on behalf of the management of the insurance holding company, without sending a dissident’s proxy circular if the solicitation is, in the prescribed circumstances, conveyed by public broadcast, speech or publication.

A person who sends a management proxy circular or dissident’s proxy circular shall concurrently send to the Superintendent a copy of it together with the form of proxy, any other documents for use in connection with the meeting and, in the case of a management proxy circular, a copy of the notice of meeting.
Subsection 789(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Superintendent shall publish in a publication generally available to the public, a notice of a decision made by the Superintendent granting an exemption under subsection (3).

Section.Text: Section 791.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the powers that may be granted by a shareholder in a form of proxy;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting proxy circulars and forms of proxy, including the form and content of those documents; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the conditions under which an insurance holding company is exempt from any of the requirements of sections 786 to 791.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to amend certain Acts in relation to financial institutions
