Section.Text: Subsection 97.47(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Dans le cas où une personne fait opposition à une cotisation ou en interjette appel en vertu de la présente partie, le ministre accepte la garantie, dont il juge satisfaisants le montant et la forme, qui lui est donnée par cette personne ou en son nom pour le paiement d’une somme en litige.
Subsection 97.48(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Any person who has been assessed under section 97.44 and who objects to the assessment may, within 90 days after the day the notice of the assessment is sent to the person, file with the Minister a notice of objection in the prescribed form and manner of filing setting out the reasons for the objection and all relevant facts.
Subsection 97.48(7) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Le ministre peut accepter l’avis d’opposition qui n’a pas été produit selon les modalités qu’il a déterminées.
Subsection 97.48(10) of the Act is replaced by the following:
After reconsidering or confirming an assessment, the Minister must send to the person objecting a written notice of the Minister’s decision by registered or certified mail.

Section.Text: Section 150 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Copies of documents, including electronic documents, made under this or any other Act of Parliament prohibiting, controlling or regulating the importation or exportation of goods, that are duly certified by an officer are admissible in evidence in any proceeding taken under this Act in the same manner as if they were the originals of such documents.
Section 164 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: The regulations made under paragraph (1)(i) for the purposes of section 3.5 may distinguish among sums according to their amount and the class of goods to which those sums relate.
Paragraphs 166(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the amount or authorizing the Minister to determine the amount of any deposit, bond or other security required to be given under this Act or the regulations; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the nature and the terms and conditions of any such deposit, bond or other security.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 166(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Any deposit, bond or other security required under this Act shall be in a form satisfactory to the Minister.

Section.Text: Sections 302 to 330 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.Text: Section 319 of the Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
Everyone who, by communicating statements, other than in private conversation, wilfully promotes antisemitism by condoning, denying or downplaying the Holocaust

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 319(4) to (6) of the Act are replaced by the following:
No person shall be convicted of an offence under subsection (2.1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if they establish that the statements communicated were true;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if, in good faith, they expressed or attempted to establish by an argument an opinion on a religious subject or an opinion based on a belief in a religious text;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the statements were relevant to any subject of public interest, the discussion of which was for the public benefit, and if on reasonable grounds they believed them to be true; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if, in good faith, they intended to point out, for the purpose of removal, matters producing or tending to produce feelings of antisemitism toward Jews.

Subsection.Text: If a person is convicted of an offence under subsection (1), (2) or (2.1) or section 318, anything by means of or in relation to which the offence was committed, on such conviction, may, in addition to any other punishment imposed, be ordered by the presiding provincial court judge or judge to be forfeited to Her Majesty in right of the province in which that person is convicted, for disposal as the Attorney General may direct.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exemption from seizure of communication facilities
Subsection.Text: Subsections 199(6) and (7) apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, to subsection (1), (2) or (2.1) or section 318.
No proceeding for an offence under subsection (2) or (2.1) shall be instituted without the consent of the Attorney General.
Subsection 319(7) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Holocaust means the planned and deliberate state-sponsored persecution and annihilation of European Jewry by the Nazis and their collaborators from 1933 to 1945; (Holocauste)

Subsection.Text: The definitions age of retirement and survivor in section 2 of the Judges Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: age of retirement of a judge or of a prothonotary means the age, fixed by law, at which the judge or prothonotary ceases to hold office; (mise à la retraite d’office)

survivor, in relation to a judge or to a prothonotary, means a person who was married to the judge or prothonotary at the time of the judge’s or prothonotary’s death or who establishes that he or she was cohabiting with the judge or prothonotary in a conjugal relationship at the time of the judge’s or prothonotary’s death and had so cohabited for a period of at least one year. (survivant)

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: prothonotary means a prothonotary of the Federal Court or a prothonotary of the Tax Court of Canada and includes a supernumerary prothonotary; (protonotaire)

Subsection.Text: The definition prothonotary in section 2 of the Act is repealed.
Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: associate judge means an associate judge of the Federal Court or an associate judge of the Tax Court of Canada and includes a supernumerary associate judge; (juge adjoint)

Section.Text: Section 2.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), sections 26 to 26.3, 34 and 39, paragraphs 40(1)(a) and (b), subsection 40(2), sections 41, 41.2 to 42, 43.1 to 56 and 57, paragraph 60(2)(b), subsections 63(1) and (2) and sections 64 to 66 also apply to a prothonotary.

Sections 41.2, 41.3, 42 and 43.1 to 52.22 do not apply to a prothonotary who made an election under the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 2 to continue to be deemed to be employed in the public service for the purposes of the Public Service Superannuation Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 25(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The yearly salaries referred to in sections 9 to 22 apply in respect of the twelve-month period beginning on April 1, 2020.

The salary annexed to an office of judge referred to in sections 9, 10, 10.2, 11 and 12 to 22 for the twelve-month period beginning on April 1, 2021, and for each subsequent twelve-month period, shall be the amount obtained by multiplying

Section.Text: Section 26.11 of the Act is replaced by the following:
In sections 26 and 26.1, judiciary includes prothonotaries.
Subsections 26.4(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Costs payable to representative of prothonotaries

Subsection.Text: The Commission may identify one representative of the prothonotaries of the Federal Court and one representative of the prothonotaries of the Tax Court of Canada participating in an inquiry of the Commission to whom costs shall be paid in accordance with this section.
The representatives identified under subsection (1) are entitled to be paid, out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, 95% of the costs determined under subsection (3) in respect of their participation.

Section.Text: The heading before section 27 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsections 27(1) and (1.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Allowance for incidental expenditures actually incurred

Subsection.Text: On and after April 1, 2020, every judge in receipt of a salary under this Act is entitled to be paid, up to a maximum of $7,500 for each year, for reasonable incidental expenditures that the fit and proper execution of the office of judge may require, to the extent that the judge has actually incurred the expenditures and is not entitled to be reimbursed for them under any other provision of this Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Allowance for incidental expenditures by prothonotaries
Subsection.Text: On and after April 1, 2020, every prothonotary is entitled to be paid, up to a maximum of $7,500 for each year, for reasonable incidental expenditures that the fit and proper execution of the office of prothonotary may require, to the extent that the prothonotary has actually incurred the expenditures and is not entitled to be reimbursed for them under any other provision of this Act.
Section 27 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

If a judge referred to in subsection (2) is required to travel for the purpose of receiving a non-elective medical or dental treatment that is required without delay and unavailable at or in the immediate vicinity of the place where the judge resides, the judge is entitled to be paid an allowance for reasonable expenses actually incurred while travelling for that purpose, to the extent that the judge may not be reimbursed for them under any other provision of this Act.
Subsection 27(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

On and after April 1, 2020, each of the following judges is entitled to be paid, as a representational allowance, reasonable travel and other expenses actually incurred by the judge or the spouse or common-law partner of the judge in discharging the special extra-judicial obligations and responsibilities that devolve on the judge, to the extent that those expenses may not be reimbursed under any other provision of this Act and their aggregate amount does not exceed in any year the maximum amount indicated below in respect of the judge:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief Justice of Canada, $25,000;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: each puisne judge of the Supreme Court of Canada, $15,000;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief Justice of the Federal Court of Appeal and each chief justice described in sections 12 to 21 as the chief justice of a province, $17,500;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: each other chief justice referred to in sections 10 to 21, $15,000;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief Justices of the Court of Appeal of Yukon, the Court of Appeal of the Northwest Territories, the Court of Appeal of Nunavut, the Supreme Court of Yukon, the Supreme Court of the Northwest Territories and the Nunavut Court of Justice, $15,000 each;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief Justice of the Court Martial Appeal Court of Canada, $15,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the Senior Judge of the Family Court, and each regional senior judge, of the Superior Court of Justice in and for the Province of Ontario, $7,500.

Section.Text: The heading before section 28 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 29:

Subsection.Text: If a prothonotary notifies the Minister of Justice of Canada of his or her election to give up regular judicial duties and hold office only as a supernumerary prothonotary, the prothonotary shall hold the office of supernumerary prothonotary from the time notice is given until he or she reaches the age of retirement, resigns or is removed from or otherwise ceases to hold office, or until the end of five years from the date of the election, whichever occurs earlier, and shall be paid the salary annexed to that office.
An election may be made under subsection (1) only by a prothonotary

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: who has continued in judicial office for at least 15 years and whose combined age and number of years in judicial office is not less than 80; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: who has attained the age of 70 years and has continued in judicial office for at least 10 years.

Subsection.Text: A prothonotary who has made the election referred to in subsection (1) shall hold himself or herself available to perform such special judicial duties as may be assigned to the prothonotary by the chief justice or the associate chief justice of the court to which he or she is appointed.
The salary of each supernumerary prothonotary is the salary annexed to the office of a prothonotary.

For the purposes of subsection (1), if a prothonotary gives notice to the Minister of Justice of Canada of the prothonotary’s election to be effective on a future day specified in the notice, being a day on which the prothonotary will be eligible to make the election, the prothonotary is, effective on that day, deemed to have elected and given notice of the election on that day.

Section.Text: Subsection 42(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In this section, judicial office means the office of a judge of a superior or county court or the office of a prothonotary.

Section.Text: The definition judicial office in subsection 43.1(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: judicial office includes the office of a prothonotary. (magistrature)

Section.Text: Subsection 50(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In this section, judicial office includes the office of a prothonotary.

Section.Text: Paragraph 69(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a judge of a superior court or a prothonotary, or

Section.Text: Section 71 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Powers, rights or duties not affected
Section.Text: Nothing in, or done or omitted to be done under the authority of, any of sections 63 to 70 affects any power, right or duty of the House of Commons, the Senate or the Governor in Council in relation to the removal from office of a judge, a prothonotary or any other person in relation to whom an inquiry may be conducted under any of those sections.
The definition federal board, commission or other tribunal in subsection 2(1) of the Federal Courts Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: federal board, commission or other tribunal means any body, person or persons having, exercising or purporting to exercise jurisdiction or powers conferred by or under an Act of Parliament or by or under an order made under a prerogative of the Crown, other than the Tax Court of Canada or any of its judges or prothonotaries, any such body constituted or established by or under a law of a province or any such person or persons appointed under or in accordance with a law of a province or under section 96 of the Constitution Act, 1867; (office fédéral)

Section.Text: Subsection 5(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Constitution of Federal Court of Appeal

Subsection.Text: The Federal Court of Appeal consists of a chief justice called the Chief Justice of the Federal Court of Appeal, who is the president of the Federal Court of Appeal, and 14 other judges.
Section 12 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
The Governor in Council may, by regulation, fix the number of prothonotaries that may be appointed under subsection (1).
For each office of prothonotary of the Federal Court, there is an additional office of supernumerary prothonotary that a prothonotary of the Federal Court may elect under the Judges Act to hold.
Section 12 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):
The Governor in Council may, by regulation, fix the workload of a supernumerary prothonotary as a percentage of the workload of a prothonotary.

Section.Text: Paragraph 4(1)(c) of the Tax Court of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: not more than 23 other judges.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 11:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may appoint as prothonotaries of the Court any fit and proper persons who are barristers or advocates in a province and who are, in the opinion of the Governor in Council, necessary for the efficient performance of the work of that court that, under the rules of the Court, is to be performed by them.
The Governor in Council may, by regulation, fix the number of prothonotaries that may be appointed under subsection (1).
For each office of prothonotary there shall be the additional office of supernumerary prothonotary that a prothonotary of the Court may elect under the Judges Act to hold.
The powers, duties and functions of the prothonotaries shall be determined by the rules of the Court.
Each prothonotary shall be paid a salary, and the allowances and annuities, provided for under the Judges Act.
The Governor in Council may, by regulation, fix the workload of a supernumerary prothonotary as a percentage of the workload of a prothonotary.
A prothonotary shall have the same immunity from liability as a judge of the Court.
A prothonotary shall hold office during good behaviour but may be removed by the Governor in Council for cause.
A prothonotary shall cease to hold office on becoming 75 years old.

Section.Text: Subsection 20(1.1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (k), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (l) and by adding the following after paragraph (l):

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: empowering a prothonotary to exercise any jurisdiction or powers, even though the jurisdiction or powers may be of a judicial nature.

Section.Text: Paragraph 22(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: three judges and one prothonotary of the Court that are designated from time to time by the Chief Justice;

Section.Text: Every reference to “prothonotary” and “prothonotaries” is replaced by a reference to “associate judge” and “associate judges”, respectively, in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the Federal Courts Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the Garnishment, Attachment and Pension Diversion Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph (a) of the definition salary in section 4, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the Judges Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the definitions age of retirement and survivor in section 2,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
