Subparagraph.Text: section 26.11,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 26.3(3),

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsections 26.4(1) and (3),

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 27(1.1),

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: the heading before section 28,

Subparagraph.Label: (xi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 30,

Subparagraph.Label: (xii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 42(4),

Subparagraph.Label: (xiii)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition judicial office in subsection 43.1(6),

Subparagraph.Label: (xiv)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 50(5),

Subparagraph.Label: (xv)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 69(1)(a), and

Subparagraph.Label: (xvi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 71;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the Tax Court of Canada Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: section 11.1 and the heading before it,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 20(1.1)(m), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 22(1)(c); and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 13(4) of the Expenditure Restraint Act.

Section.Text: For greater certainty, every person who, immediately before the day on which this section comes into force, holds office as prothonotary of the Federal Court, supernumerary prothonotary of the Federal Court, prothonotary of the Tax Court of Canada or supernumerary prothonotary of the Tax Court of Canada continues in office as associate judge of the Federal Court, supernumerary associate judge of the Federal Court, associate judge of the Tax Court of Canada or supernumerary associate judge of the Tax Court of Canada, as the case may be.

For greater certainty, for the purposes of the Judges Act, nothing in section 371 of this Act affects the number of years during which a person who held office as prothonotary, as defined in that Act as it read immediately before the day on which this section comes into force, has continued in judicial office.

Despite paragraph 22(1)(c) of the Tax Court of Canada Act, the rules committee referred to in that Act may exercise its powers and perform its duties without the designation, as a member of the rules committee, of a person appointed under section 11.1 of that Act until a person is first appointed under that section.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (2) to (10) apply if Bill C-9, introduced in the 1st session of the 44th Parliament and entitled An Act to amend the Judges Act (in this section referred to as the “other Act”), receives royal assent.
The reference to “Minister of Justice of Canada” is replaced with “Minister” in section 30 of the Judges Act.
If section 2 of the other Act comes into force before subparagraph 371(c)(ii) of this Act, then subsection 2.1(1) of the Judges Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection (2), sections 26 to 26.‍3, 34 and 39, paragraphs 40(1)‍(a) and (b), subsection 40(2), sections 41, 41.‍2 to 42, 43.‍1 to 56 and 57, paragraph 60(2)‍(b) and Part IV also apply to a prothonotary.
If subparagraph 371(c)(ii) of this Act comes into force before section 2 of the other Act, then subsection 2.1(1) of the Judges Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection (2), sections 26 to 26.‍3, 34 and 39, paragraphs 40(1)‍(a) and (b), subsection 40(2), sections 41, 41.‍2 to 42, 43.‍1 to 56 and 57, paragraph 60(2)‍(b) and Part IV also apply to an associate judge.

If section 2 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as subparagraph 371(c)(ii) of this Act, then that subparagraph 371(c)(ii) is deemed to have come into force before that section 2 and subsection (4) applies as a consequence.
If section 10 of the other Act comes into force before section 362 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 362 and section 363 of this Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 79 of the Judges Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: In this Division, judicial office includes the office of a prothonotary.

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 371(c) of this Act is amended by adding “and” after subparagraph (xiv) and by replacing subparagraphs (xv) and (xvi) with the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (xv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 79;

Subsection.Text: If section 362 of this Act comes into force before section 10 of the other Act and that section 10 comes into force before subparagraph 371(c)(xv) of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: section 79 of the Judges Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: In this Division, judicial office includes the office of a prothonotary.

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 371(c) of this Act is amended by adding “and” after subparagraph (xiv) and by replacing subparagraphs (xv) and (xvi) with the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (xv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 79;

Subsection.Text: If section 10 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 362 of this Act, then that section 10 is deemed to have come into force before that section 362 and subsection (6) applies as a consequence.
If paragraph 371(c) of this Act comes into force before section 12 of the other Act, then section 79 of the Judges Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: In this Division, judicial office includes the office of an associate judge.

Subsection.Text: If section 12 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as paragraph 371(c) of this Act, then that paragraph 371(c) is deemed to have come into force before that section 12 and subsection (9) applies as a consequence.

Section.Text: Subsections 333(3) and (4) and sections 371 to 373 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 10.3(1)(a) of the French version of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: les catégories auxquelles le paragraphe 10.1(1) s’applique;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 10.3(1)(h) to (j) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the basis on which an eligible foreign national may be ranked;

Paragraph.Label: (h.1)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (1.01), the establishment of categories of eligible foreign nationals for the purposes of ranking, which groupings may consist of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: all eligible foreign nationals,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: eligible foreign nationals who are eligible to be members of a class referred to in an instruction given under paragraph (a), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: eligible foreign nationals who are eligible to be members of a category established in an instruction given under paragraph (h.2);

Paragraph.Label: (h.2)
Paragraph.Text: the establishment of categories for the purposes of ranking and the criteria for eligibility to be a member of a category;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the rank within a grouping that an eligible foreign national must occupy to be invited to make an application in respect of a class referred to in an instruction given under paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the number of invitations that may be issued within a specified period in respect of a grouping;

Paragraph.Label: (j.1)
Paragraph.Text: the class referred to in an instruction given under paragraph (a) in respect of which an eligible foreign national who is invited to make an application must apply, if the foreign national is eligible to be a member of more than one class;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 10.3(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An instruction given under paragraph (1)(h.1) must not establish a category in respect of which a public consultation process referred to in subsection 10.5(1) has not been given the opportunity to provide advice and recommendations.

If the Minister establishes a category in an instruction given under paragraph (1)(h.2), the Minister shall set out, in the instruction, the economic goal that the Minister seeks to support in establishing the category.
For greater certainty, an instruction given under paragraph (1)(j) may provide that the number of invitations that may be issued in any specified period in respect of a grouping be zero.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 10.4:

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of establishing categories of eligible foreign nationals under subparagraph 10.3(1)(h.1)(iii), the Minister must engage in a public consultation process with stakeholders, including provinces and territories, industry, unions, employers, workers, worker advocacy groups, settlement provider organizations and immigration researchers and practitioners, to obtain information, advice and recommendations in respect of the labour market conditions, including occupations expected to face shortage conditions, as well as on how categories can be formed to meet economic goals.
The advice and recommendations from the public consultation process must be based on written submissions provided by relevant industry members and stakeholders.

The Minister shall cause to be tabled before each House of Parliament, not later than the fifth sitting day of that House after January 31 following the end of each fiscal year, a report containing the list of the categories of eligible foreign nationals established in an instruction made under paragraph 10.3(1)(h.1) and the selection criteria and the process applied for the establishment of those categories.

After it is tabled, the report stands referred to the committee of the Senate, the House of Commons or both Houses of Parliament that may be designated or established for the purpose of reviewing the report.
Subsection 11.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Visa or other document not to be issued

Subsection.Text: An officer may not issue a visa or other document in respect of an application for permanent residence to a foreign national who was issued an invitation under Division 0.1 to make that application if — at the time the invitation was issued or at the time the officer received their application — the foreign national

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: did not meet the criteria set out in an instruction given under paragraph 10.3(1)(e);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: did not have the qualifications on the basis of which they were ranked under an instruction given under paragraph 10.3(1)(h) and were issued the invitation; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: did not meet the criteria for membership in a category that was established in an instruction given under paragraph 10.3(1)(h.2), if they were issued an invitation on the basis that they were eligible to be a member of that category.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 11.2(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the foreign national did not meet the criteria set out in an instruction given under paragraph 10.3(1)(e) — or they did not have the qualifications on the basis of which they were ranked under an instruction given under paragraph 10.3(1)(h) or did not meet the criteria for membership in a category that was established in an instruction given under paragraph 10.3(1)(h.2) — because the foreign national’s birthday occurred after the invitation was issued; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 11.2(2)(b) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (i) and by adding the following after that subparagraph:

Subparagraph.Label: (i.1)
Subparagraph.Text: they met the criteria for membership of a category established in an instruction given under paragraph 10.3(1)(h.2), if they were issued the invitation on the basis that they were eligible to be a member of that category, and

Section.Text: Subsection 94(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: any instructions given under paragraph 10.3(1)(h.2) that establish a category of eligible foreign nationals, the economic goal sought to be supported in establishing the category and the number of foreign nationals invited to make an application for permanent residence in respect of the category;

Section.Text: Subparagraph (c)(i.1) of the definition income in section 2 of the Old Age Security Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i.1)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the payment under the program referred to in section 275 of the Budget Implementation Act, 2021, No. 1,

Section.Text: This Division is deemed to have come into force on June 29, 2021.
Subparagraph 6(1)(b)(ii) of the Canada Emergency Response Benefit Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: benefits, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Employment Insurance Act, or an employment insurance emergency response benefit referred to in section 153.7 of that Act,

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 14:

Section.MarginalNote: Receipt of benefits, allowances or money

Subsection.Text: If, for any four-week period, the Minister determines that a worker received an income support payment for which they were not eligible by reason only that they received one or more payments of benefits, allowances or money referred to in subparagraph 6(1)(b)(ii) or (iii), the Minister is deemed to have determined under subsection 12(2) that the amount that the worker must repay under subsection 12(1) is the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the number of weeks for which the worker received such benefits, allowances or money during that four-week period.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of an employment insurance emergency response benefit received by the worker if the Canada Employment Insurance Commission informs the Minister that subsection (1) should not apply in respect of that benefit and, if the Commission does so, the worker is, despite subparagraph 6(1)(b)(ii), deemed to have been eligible to receive the income support payment.

Section.Text: The Canada Emergency Student Benefit Act is amended by adding the following after section 15:

Section.MarginalNote: Receipt of benefits, allowances or money

Subsection.Text: If, for any four-week period, the Minister determines that a student received a Canada emergency student benefit for which they were not eligible by reason only that they received one or more payments of benefits, allowances or money referred to in subparagraph 6(1)(b)(ii) or (iii), the Minister is deemed to have determined under subsection 13(2) that the amount that the worker must repay under subsection 13(1) is, despite subsection 13(1), the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: $2,000, in the case of a student with a dependant or a student with a disability, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: $1,250, in any other case; and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the number of weeks for which the student received such benefits, allowances or money during that four-week period.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of an employment insurance emergency response benefit received by the student if the Canada Employment Insurance Commission informs the Minister that subsection (1) should not apply in respect of that benefit and, if the Commission does so, the student is, despite subparagraph 6(1)(b)(ii), deemed to have been eligible to receive the income support payment.

Section.Text: Section 153.9 of the Employment Insurance Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.Text: If, for any week, a claimant received an employment insurance emergency response benefit for which they were not eligible by reason only of paragraph (2)(c), the claimant, despite that paragraph, is deemed to have been eligible for the benefit unless the Commission has, under subsection 15(2) of the Canada Emergency Response Benefit Act, informed the Minister, as defined in section 2 of that Act, that subsection 15(1) of that Act should not apply in respect of the claimant.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Receipt of Canada emergency student benefit
Subsection.Text: If, for any week, a claimant received an employment insurance emergency response benefit for which they were not eligible by reason only of paragraph (2)(d), the claimant, despite that paragraph, is deemed to have been eligible for the benefit unless the Commission has, under subsection 15.1(2) of the Canada Emergency Student Benefit Act, informed the Minister, as defined in section 2 of that Act, that subsection 15.1(1) of that Act should not apply in respect of the claimant.

Section.Text: Section 382 is deemed to have come into force on March 15, 2020.

Subsection.Text: The definition employment benefits in subsection 2(1) of the Employment Insurance Act is repealed.
The definition benefits in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: benefits means unemployment benefits payable under Part I, VII.1 or VIII; (prestation)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: employment support measure means a measure established under section 59; (mesure de soutien à l’emploi)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 5(1)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: employment in Canada of an individual as the sponsor or co-ordinator of an employment support measure other than one referred to in paragraph 59(c) or (d).

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 5(6)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any employment provided under regulations made under section 24 or under an employment support measure other than one referred to in paragraph 59(c) or (d).

Section.Text: Paragraph 8(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: receiving assistance under an employment support measure other than one referred to in paragraph 59(c) or (d); or

Section.Text: Subsection 19(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Earnings and allowances from employment support measures, courses and programs
Subsection.Text: Earnings from employment under an employment support measure other than one referred to in paragraph 59(c) or (d) and earnings or allowances payable to a claimant for attending a course or program of instruction or training shall not be deducted under this section except in accordance with the regulations.

Section.Text: The heading before section 25 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Courses, Programs and Employment Support Measures

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 25(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: attending a course or program of instruction or training — at the claimant’s own expense, under an employment support measure referred to in paragraph 59(a) or under a measure that is the subject of an agreement under section 63 — to which the Commission, or an authority that the Commission designates, has referred the claimant; or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 25(1)(b)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for which assistance has been provided for the claimant under a prescribed employment support measure — other than one referred to in paragraph 59(a) or (c) — or a prescribed measure that is the subject of an agreement under section 63, and

Section.Text: Section 26 of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this Part, Part IV, the Income Tax Act and the Canada Pension Plan, benefits paid to a claimant while employed under an employment support measure — other than one referred to in paragraph 59(c) or (d) — or under a measure that is the subject of an agreement under section 63 are not earnings from employment.
Paragraph 27(1.1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Commission or an authority that the Commission designates has, with the agreement of the claimant, referred the claimant to a course or program of instruction or training or to any other employment activity for which assistance has been provided under an employment support measure other than one referred to in paragraph 59(c); and

Section.Text: The heading of Part II of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Employment Support Measures and National Employment Service

Section.Text: Section 56 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The purpose of this Part is to help maintain a sustainable employment insurance system through the establishment of employment support measures and the maintenance of a national employment service.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 57(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Employment support measures under this Part shall be established in accordance with the following guidelines:
Paragraph 57(1)(a) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: l’harmonisation des mesures avec les projets d’emploi provinciaux en vue d’éviter tout double emploi et tout chevauchement;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 57(1)(d.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: availability of assistance under the measures in either official language where there is significant demand for that assistance in that language;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 57(1)(e) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: commitment by persons receiving assistance under the measures to

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 57(1)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: implementation of the measures within a framework for evaluating their success in assisting persons to obtain or keep employment.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 57(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

To give effect to the purpose and guidelines of this Part, the Commission shall work in concert with provincial governments and consult with workers and employers to align employment support measures with labour market needs.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 58(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an insured person who requests assistance under an employment support measure and, when requesting the assistance, is a person for whom a benefit period is established or whose benefit period has ended within the previous 60 months or a person who paid, in at least 3 of the last 10 years, employee’s premiums that did not entitle them to a refund under subsection 96(4); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an insured person who requests assistance under an employment support measure and, when requesting assistance, is a person who was in receipt of the employment insurance emergency response benefit within the previous 60 months.

Section.Text: Section 59 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Commission may establish employment support measures to help insured participants and other workers, including workers in groups underrepresented in the labour market, to obtain or keep employment, including measures to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provide insured participants with courses or programs of instruction or training;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provide insured participants with employment opportunities or provide employment support;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provide workers with employment assistance services; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: support research, innovation or partnerships related to helping workers to prepare for, obtain or keep employment and to be productive participants in the labour market.

Section.Text: Subsections 60(4) and (5) of the Act are repealed.
Sections 61 and 62 of the Act are replaced by the following:
The Commission may, in accordance with terms and conditions approved by the Treasury Board, provide financial assistance for the purpose of implementing employment support measures.

Section.MarginalNote: Agreements for administering employment support measures
Section.Text: The Commission may, with the approval of the Minister, enter into an agreement or arrangement for the administration of employment support measures on its behalf by a department, board or agency of the Government of Canada, another government or government agency in Canada or any other public or private organization.
Paragraphs 63(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any costs of measures implemented by the government, government agency or organization that are consistent with the purpose and guidelines of this Part; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any administration costs that the government, government agency or organization incurs in implementing the measures.

Section.Text: Section 64 of the Act is replaced by the following:
A decision of the Commission made in relation to employment support measures, other than a decision under section 65.1, is not subject to review under section 112.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 75(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: received as repayments of overpayments by the Commission under section 61 for employment support measures authorized by Part II;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 75(e) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: e)
Paragraph.Text: reçues à titre de remboursement de versements excédentaires faits par la Commission aux termes d’accords conclus au titre de l’article 63;

Section.Text: Paragraph 77(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts paid under section 61 for employment support measures;

Section.Text: The Employment Insurance Act, as it read immediately before the day on which this Division comes into force, continues to apply to agreements or arrangements entered into under Part II of that Act that are in force on that day.

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendment to the Income Tax Act

Section.Text: Subparagraph 56(1)(r)(iii) of the Income Tax Act is amended by adding “and” at the end of clause (A) and by repealing clause (B).
Subsections 12(2.3) to (2.5) of the Employment Insurance Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: General maximum — exception for seasonal workers
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (2), the maximum number of weeks for which benefits may be paid in a benefit period to a claimant because of a reason other than those mentioned in subsection (3) shall be determined in accordance with the table set out in Schedule V by reference to the regional rate of unemployment that applies to the claimant and the number of hours of insurable employment of the claimant in their qualifying period if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the following conditions are met:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the date on which a benefit period for the claimant is established falls within the period beginning on September 26, 2021 and ending on October 28, 2023,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: on the date on which the benefit period is established, the claimant is ordinarily resident in a region described in Schedule VI,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the 260 weeks before the date on which the benefit period referred to in subparagraph (i) begins, at least three benefit periods were established during which regular benefits were paid or payable, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: at least two of the benefit periods referred to in subparagraph (iii) began around the same time of year as the benefit period referred to in subparagraph (i) began; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the conditions referred to in subparagraphs (a)(i) and (ii) are met and the claimant had met the criteria set out in paragraphs 77.992(2)(b) to (d) of the Employment Insurance Regulations — taking into account subsections 77.992(3) and (4) of those Regulations — in respect of a benefit period established for the claimant on a date within the period referred to in paragraph 77.992(2)(a) of those Regulations.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subparagraph (2.3)(a)(iii), a claimant’s benefit period established before the beginning of the 260-week period is considered to have been established within the 260-week period if the claimant received a notification of payment or non-payment with respect to any week that falls within that 260-week period.

For the purposes of subparagraph (2.3)(a)(iv), a benefit period in a previous year is considered to have begun around the same time of year if it began during the period that begins eight weeks before and ends eight weeks after the week that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 52 weeks before the first week of the benefit period referred to in subparagraph (2.3)(a)(i);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 104 weeks before the first week of the benefit period referred to in subparagraph (2.3)(a)(i);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: 156 weeks before the first week of the benefit period referred to in subparagraph (2.3)(a)(i);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: 208 weeks before the first week of the benefit period referred to in subparagraph (2.3)(a)(i); or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: 260 weeks before the first week of the benefit period referred to in subparagraph (2.3)(a)(i).

Section.Text: Schedule V to the Act is replaced by the Schedule V set out in Schedule 3 to this Act.
Schedule VI to the Act is amended by replacing the reference after the heading “SCHEDULE VI” with the following:

Schedule V to the Employment Insurance Act, as it read immediately before September 25, 2022, continues to apply in respect of a claimant whose benefit period, as those terms are defined in subsection 2(1) of that Act, begins before September 25, 2022.
The Budget Implementation Act, 2021, No. 1 is amended by adding the following after section 350:

Subsection.Text: Subsection 35(6), paragraph 35(7)(g) and section 36 of the former Regulations continue to apply in respect of a claimant’s earnings if, but for this subsection, the earnings would be allocated under subsection 36(9) or (10) of the new Regulations to a number of weeks the first week of which falls within the period beginning on September 26, 2021 and ending on September 24, 2022.
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: claimant has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Employment Insurance Act. (prestataire)
earnings means the earnings referred to in subsections 36(9) and (10) of the new Regulations. (rémunération)
former Regulations means the Employment Insurance Regulations as they read immediately before September 25, 2022. (ancien règlement)
new Regulations means the Employment Insurance Regulations as they read on September 25, 2022. (nouveau règlement)

Subsection.Text: Subsections (2) to (4) apply if Bill C-8, introduced in the 1st session of the 44th Parliament and entitled the Economic and Fiscal Update Implementation Act, 2021 (in this section referred to as the “other Act”), receives royal assent.
If section 47 of the other Act comes into force before section 408 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sections 408 and 410 of this Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph 12(2.3)(a)(i) of the Employment Insurance Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the date on which a benefit period for the claimant is established falls within the period beginning on September 26, 2021 and ending on October 28, 2023,

Subsection.Text: If section 408 of this Act comes into force before section 47 of the other Act, then sections 47 and 48 of the other Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed.
If section 47 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 408 of this Act, then sections 47 and 48 of the other Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed.
Sections 409 and 411 come into force, or are deemed to have come into force, on September 25, 2022.
Section 412 comes into force on the day on which this Act receives royal assent or, if that day is after September 25, 2022, is deemed to have come into force on September 25, 2022.

Section.Text: The definition contributory period in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Pension Plan is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: contributory period of a contributor has, subject to paragraph 44(2)(b) and subsections 44(5) and 56(5), the meaning assigned by section 49; (période cotisable)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 44(1)(h) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a post-retirement disability benefit shall be paid to a beneficiary of a retirement pension who has not reached 65 years of age and is disabled if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the beneficiary has made base contributions for not less than the minimum qualifying period and that period ends after 2018,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the beneficiary is a contributor to whom a post-retirement disability benefit would have been payable at the time the contributor is deemed to have become disabled if an application for a post-retirement disability benefit had been received before the application was actually received, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the beneficiary is a contributor to whom a post-retirement disability benefit would have been payable at the time the contributor is deemed to have become disabled if a division of unadjusted pensionable earnings that was made under section 55 or 55.1 had not been made.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 44(4) of the Act before paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Calculation of minimum qualifying period — post-retirement disability benefit
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (1)(h) and, if a post-retirement disability benefit is payable to a contributor, paragraph (1)(e), the contributor is deemed to have made base contributions for not less than the minimum qualifying period only if the contributor has made base contributions during their contributory period on earnings that are not less than the contributor’s basic exemption, calculated without regard to subsection 20(2),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for at least four of the last six calendar years included either wholly or partly in the contributor’s contributory period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for at least 25 calendar years included either wholly or partly in the contributor’s contributory period, of which at least three are in the last six calendar years included either wholly or partly in the contributor’s contributory period; or

Subsection.Text: Section 44 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):
For the purposes of subsection (4), the contributory period of a contributor is the period

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: commencing when they reach 18 years of age, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ending with the month in which they are determined to have become disabled for the purpose of paragraph (1)(h),

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any month that was excluded from the contributor’s contributory period under this Act or under a provincial pension plan by reason of disability, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to any benefits payable under this Act for any month after December 1977, any month for which the contributor was a family allowance recipient in a year for which the contributor’s base unadjusted pensionable earnings are less than the contributor’s basic exemption for the year, calculated without regard to subsection 20(2).

Section.Text: The portion of paragraph 49(b) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where a benefit other than a disability pension or a post-retirement disability benefit commences after the end of 1986, with the earliest of

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph (a) of the description of G in section 51.1 of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the lesser of 1 and the number determined by the formula

Subsection.Text: The description of M7 in section 51.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the number of months in the contributor’s first additional contributory period in the year in which they were deemed to have become disabled that are before the month following the month in which they were deemed to have become disabled; and

Subsection.Text: Section 51.1 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 51.1(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Year in which first additional contributory period begins
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of the descriptions of A to F in subsection (1), if the contributor’s first additional contributory period begins in the six years before the year in which they were deemed to have become disabled, the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year in which their first additional contributory period begins is replaced by the prorated portion determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year in which the contributor’s first additional contributory period begins; and
is the number of months in that year that are included in the contributor’s first additional contributory period.

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph (a) of the description of G in section 51.2 of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the lesser of 1 and the number determined by the formula

Subsection.Text: The description of M7 in section 51.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the number of months in the contributor’s second additional contributory period in the year in which they were deemed to have become disabled that are before the month following the month in which they were deemed to have become disabled; and

Subsection.Text: Section 51.2 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 51.2(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Year in which second additional contributory period begins
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of the descriptions of A to F in subsection (1), if the contributor’s second additional contributory period begins in the six years before the year in which they were deemed to have become disabled, the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year in which their second additional contributory period begins is replaced by the prorated portion determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year in which the contributor’s second additional contributory period begins; and
is the number of months in that year that are included in the contributor’s second additional contributory period.

Section.Text: Section 53.3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Year in which first additional contributory period begins
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of the descriptions of A to E in subsection (1), if the contributor’s first additional contributory period begins in the five years before the year in which they became a family allowance recipient, the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year in which their first additional contributory period begins is replaced by the prorated portion determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year in which the contributor’s first additional contributory period begins; and
is the number of months in that year that are included in the contributor’s first additional contributory period.

Section.Text: Section 53.4 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Year in which second additional contributory period begins
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of the descriptions of A to E in subsection (1), if the contributor’s second additional contributory period begins in the five years before the year in which they became a family allowance recipient,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year in which their second additional contributory period begins is replaced by the prorated portion determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year in which the contributor’s second additional contributory period begins, and
is the number of months in that year that are included in the contributor’s second additional contributory period; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Year’s Additional Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year in which their second additional contributory period begins is replaced by the prorated portion determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the Year’s Additional Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year in which the contributor’s second additional contributory period begins, and
is the number of months in that year that are included in the contributor’s second additional contributory period.

Section.MarginalNote: Non-application — subsection 114(2) of Canada Pension Plan

Subsection.Text: Subsection 114(2) of the Canada Pension Plan does not apply in respect of the amendments to that Act contained in this Division.
This Division comes into force, in accordance with subsection 114(4) of the Canada Pension Plan, on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Canada Labour Code

Subsection.Text: Subsection 7(1) of An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Canada Labour Code is amended by replacing the subsection 239(1.2) that it enacts with the following:
Subject to subsection (1.21) and the regulations, an employee earns, as of the first day on which this subsection applies to the employee,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: after completing 30 days of continuous employment with an employer, three days of medical leave of absence with pay; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: following the period of 30 days referred to in paragraph (a), at the beginning of each month after completing one month of continuous employment with the employer, one day of medical leave of absence with pay.

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, an employee is entitled to earn up to 10 days of medical leave of absence with pay in a calendar year.
Subsection 7(1) of the Act is amended by replacing the subsection 239(1.4) that it enacts with the following:

Subject to the regulations, each day of medical leave of absence with pay that an employee does not take in a calendar year is to be carried forward to January 1 of the following calendar year and decreases, by one, the maximum number of days that can be earned in that calendar year under subsection (1.21).
Subsection 7(1) of the Act is amended by replacing the subsections 239(1.6) and (2) that it enacts with the following:

The employer may, in writing and no later than 15 days after the return to work of an employee who has taken a medical leave of absence of at least five consecutive days, require the employee to provide a certificate issued by a health care practitioner certifying that the employee was incapable of working for the period of their medical leave of absence.
Subsection 7(2) of the Act is amended by amending the subsection 239(13) that it enacts by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a) and by replacing paragraph (b) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: modifying subsection (1.2), (1.21) or (1.4) if, in the opinion of the Governor in Council, employees or classes of employees will, despite the modification, earn periods of medical leave of absence with pay that are substantially equivalent to the period provided for in subsection (1.21); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: providing for employees or classes of employees to earn periods of medical leave of absence with pay other than in accordance with subsection (1.2) if, in the opinion of the Governor in Council, the periods of medical leave of absence with pay are substantially equivalent to the period provided for in subsection (1.21).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 7(2) of the Act is amended by adding, after the subsection 239(13) that it enacts, the following:
Section 189 applies for the purposes of this Division.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7:
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 239:

The provisions of this Division respecting the medical leave of absence with pay apply to an employer and its employees beginning on the first day on which, as of the day on which this section comes into force, the employer has 100 or more employees, even if the number of employees falls below 100 after that first day.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 8(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Sections 6 and 7 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, but no later than December 1, 2022.
Section 8 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
Section 7.1 comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Heading.TitleText: Related Amendment to the Canada Labour Code

Section.Text: Section 239.001 of the Canada Labour Code is repealed.

Paragraph 206.6(1)(a) of the Canada Labour Code, as it read immediately before the day on which section 6 of An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Canada Labour Code, chapter 27 of the Statutes of Canada, 2021, comes into force, continues to apply to every employer and its employees to which section 239.001 of the Canada Labour Code, as enacted by section 7.1 of An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Canada Labour Code, chapter 27 of the Statutes of Canada, 2021, does not apply, until the day on which section 426 of this Act comes into force.

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Canada Labour Code, chapter 27 of the Statutes of Canada, 2021.
If section 7 of the other Act comes into force before section 423 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sections 423 to 427 and 429 of this Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 239(1.2) of the Canada Labour Code is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (1.21) and the regulations, an employee earns, as of the day on which this subsection comes into force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: after completing 30 days of continuous employment with an employer, three days of medical leave of absence with pay; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: following the period of 30 days referred to in paragraph (a), at the beginning of each month after completing one month of continuous employment with the employer, one day of medical leave of absence with pay.

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, an employee is entitled to earn up to 10 days of medical leave of absence with pay in a calendar year.

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 239(1.4) of the Canada Labour Code is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, each day of medical leave of absence with pay that an employee does not take in a calendar year is to be carried forward to January 1 of the following calendar year and decreases, by one, the maximum number of days that can be earned in that calendar year under subsection (1.21).

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 239(1.6) and (2) of the Canada Labour Code are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The employer may, in writing and no later than 15 days after the return to work of an employee who has taken a medical leave of absence of at least five consecutive days, require the employee to provide a certificate issued by a health care practitioner certifying that the employee was incapable of working for the period of their medical leave of absence.

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 239(13) of the Canada Labour Code is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a) and by replacing paragraph (b) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: modifying subsection (1.2), (1.21) or (1.4) if, in the opinion of the Governor in Council, employees or classes of employees will, despite the modification, earn periods of medical leave of absence with pay that are substantially equivalent to the period provided for in subsection (1.21); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: providing for employees or classes of employees to earn periods of medical leave of absence with pay other than in accordance with subsection (1.2) if, in the opinion of the Governor in Council, the periods of medical leave of absence with pay are substantially equivalent to the period provided for in subsection (1.21).

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: section 239 of the Canada Labour Code is amended by adding the following after subsection (13):

Subsection.Text: Section 189 applies for the purposes of this Division.
If section 423 of this Act comes into force on the same day as section 7 of the other Act, then that section 423 is deemed to have come into force before that section 7.

Section.Text: Section 426 comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsection 21.1(7) of the Canada Business Corporations Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: This section does not apply to a corporation

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is a reporting issuer or an émetteur assujetti under an Act of the legislature of a province relating to the regulation of securities;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any of the securities of which are listed and posted for trading on a designated stock exchange, as defined in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that is a member of a prescribed class.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 21.2:

Subsection.Text: A corporation to which section 21.1 applies shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on an annual basis, send to the Director the information in its register of individuals with significant control over the corporation, in the form and within the period that the Director fixes; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within 15 days after the day on which it records information under subsection 21.1(3), send the information to the Director, in the form that the Director fixes.

Subsection.Text: On or after the date shown on a certificate referred to in section 8, subsection 185(4) or 187(4), a corporation to which section 21.1 applies shall send to the Director the information referred to in paragraphs 21.1(1)(a) to (f) in relation to individuals with significant control over the corporation, in the form and within the period that the Director fixes.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Period for keeping and producing information
Subsection.Text: The Director is not required to keep or produce any information received under subsection (1) or (2) after the end of the six-year period following the day on which it is received.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 21.3:

The Director may provide all or part of the information received under section 21.21 to an investigative body referred to in subsection 21.31(2), the Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada or any prescribed entity.
Section 266 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person who has paid the required fee is entitled during usual business hours to examine a document required by this Act or the regulations to be sent to the Director, except any information sent under section 21.21 and a report sent to the Director under subsection 230(2), and to make copies of or extracts from it.

The Director shall furnish any person with a copy, extract, certified copy or certified extract of a document required by this Act or the regulations to be sent to the Director, except any information sent under section 21.21 and a report sent under subsection 230(2).

Section.Text: On the first day on which both section 44 of An Act to amend the Canada Business Corporations Act, the Canada Cooperatives Act, the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act and the Competition Act, chapter 8 of the Statutes of Canada, 2018, and section 433 of this Act are in force, section 266 of the Canada Business Corporations Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person who has paid the required fee is entitled during usual business hours to examine and make copies of or take extracts from a document, except any information sent under section 21.21 and a report sent to the Director under subsection 230(2), that is required to be sent to the Director under this Act or that was required to be sent to a person performing a similar function under prior legislation.
The Director shall, on request, provide any person with a copy, extract, certified copy or certified extract of a document that may be examined under subsection (1).

Section.Text: This Division, except sections 430 and 434, comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The definition property in section 2 of the Special Economic Measures Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: property means any type of property, whether real or personal or immovable or movable, or tangible or intangible or corporeal or incorporeal, and includes money, funds, currency, digital assets and virtual currency; (bien)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 3:

The purpose of this Act is to enable the Government of Canada to take economic measures against certain persons in circumstances where an international organization of states or association of states of which Canada is a member calls on its members to do so, a grave breach of international peace and security has occurred, gross and systematic human rights violations have been committed in a foreign state or acts of significant corruption involving a national of a foreign state have been committed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 4(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Governor in Council may, if the Governor in Council is of the opinion that any of the circumstances described in subsection (1.1) has occurred,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make any orders or regulations with respect to the restriction or prohibition of any of the activities referred to in subsection (2) in relation to a foreign state that the Governor in Council considers necessary; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: by order, cause to be seized or restrained in the manner set out in the order any property situated in Canada that is owned — or that is held or controlled, directly or indirectly — by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a foreign state,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any person in that foreign state, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a national of that foreign state who does not ordinarily reside in Canada.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 4(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any dealing by any person in Canada or Canadian outside Canada in any property, wherever situated, that is owned — or that is held or controlled, directly or indirectly — by that foreign state, any person in that foreign state, or a national of that foreign state who does not ordinarily reside in Canada;

Subsection.Text: Subsections 4(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:
The Governor in Council may, by order, authorize the Minister to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: issue to any person in Canada or Canadian outside Canada a permit to carry out a specified activity or transaction, or class of activity or transaction, that is restricted or prohibited under this Act or any order or regulations made under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: issue a general permit allowing any person in Canada or Canadian outside Canada to carry out a specified activity or transaction, or class of activity or transaction, that is restricted or prohibited under this Act or any order or regulations made under this Act.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may issue a permit or general permit, subject to any terms and conditions that are, in the opinion of the Minister, consistent with this Act and any order or regulations made under this Act.

Section.Text: Section 5 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Any costs incurred by or on behalf of Her Majesty in right of Canada in relation to the seizure or restraint of property under an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b) or the disposal of property forfeited under section 5.4 are the liability of the owner of the property and constitute a debt due to Her Majesty in right of Canada that may be recovered in any court of competent jurisdiction.

Subsection.Text: A person whose property is the subject of an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b) may, unless the property is the subject of a forfeiture order, apply at any time to the Minister in writing to request that the property cease being the subject of the order made under that paragraph.
On receipt of an application, the Minister must decide whether there are reasonable grounds to recommend to the Governor in Council that the property cease to be the subject of the order.

Section.Text: All secured and unsecured rights and interests in any property that is the subject of an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b) that are held by a person are entitled to the same ranking that they would have been entitled to had the order not been made, unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the foreign state identified in the order,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person in that state, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a national of that state who does not ordinarily reside in Canada; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the property is forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada under section 5.4.

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in sections 5.4 to 5.6.

Definition.Text: judge means a judge of a superior court of the province where property described in an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b) is situated. (juge)
Minister means the Minister responsible under section 6 for the administration of an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b). (ministre)

Subsection.Text: On application by the Minister, a judge shall order that the property that is the subject of the application be forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada if the judge determines, based on the evidence presented, that the property

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is described in an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is owned by the person referred to in that order or is held or controlled, directly or indirectly, by that person.

Subsection.Text: Before making the order in relation to the property, the court shall require notice to be given to any person who, in the court’s opinion, appears to have an interest in or right to the property, and the court may hear any such person.
The notice shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: be given in the manner that the court directs or that may be specified in the rules of the court;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: specify the period that the court considers reasonable or that may be set out in the rules of the court during which a person may, before the order in relation to the property is made, make an application to the court asserting their interest in or right to the property; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: set out a description of the property.

Subsection.Text: Any person — other than a person referred to in any of subparagraphs 5.2(a)(i) to (iii) — who claims an interest in or right to property that is forfeited to Her Majesty under subsection (1) may, within 30 days after the day on which the property is forfeited, apply in writing to a judge for an order declaring that their interest or right is not affected by the forfeiture, declaring the nature and extent of the interest or right and directing the Minister to pay to the person an amount equal to the value of their interest or right.

Section.Text: If the property that is the subject of a forfeiture order consists of all of the shares of a corporation, the corporation is deemed not to be a Crown corporation as defined in subsection 83(1) of the Financial Administration Act.

After consulting with the Minister of Finance and the Minister of Foreign Affairs, the Minister may — at the times and in the manner, and on any terms and conditions, that the Minister considers appropriate — pay out of the Proceeds Account, as defined in section 2 of the Seized Property Management Act, amounts not exceeding the net proceeds from the disposition of property forfeited under section 5.4, but only for any of the following purposes:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the reconstruction of a foreign state adversely affected by a grave breach of international peace and security;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the restoration of international peace and security; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the compensation of victims of a grave breach of international peace and security, gross and systematic human rights violations or acts of significant corruption.

Section.Text: Subsection 6(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Sous réserve du paragraphe (2), le ministre des Affaires étrangères est chargé de l’exécution et du contrôle d’application de la présente loi.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 6:

The following persons may assist the Minister in matters relating to the making, administration or enforcement of an order or regulation referred to in subsection 4(1) and, for that purpose, may collect information from and disclose information to each other:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Foreign Affairs;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Finance;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Public Works and Government Services;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the Director of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief of the Communications Security Establishment;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the President of the Canada Border Services Agency; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the Superintendent of Financial Institutions.

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police may assist the Minister in matters related to the making of an order under paragraph 4(1)(b), the seizure or restraint of any property that is the subject of such an order or the making of an application for forfeiture of the property under section 5.4 and, for that purpose, may collect information from and disclose information to the persons referred to in section 6.1.
For greater certainty, nothing in subsection (1) is to be construed as affecting the powers of a peace officer that are conferred under legislation or the common law.

The Minister of Foreign Affairs may require any person to provide to that Minister any information that that Minister believes on reasonable grounds is relevant for the purposes of the making, administration or enforcement of an order or regulation referred to in subsection 4(1).
Every person who is required to provide information under subsection (1) shall comply with the requirement within the time and in the form and manner specified by that Minister.

Section.Text: Subsection 7(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every order and regulation made under paragraph 4(1)(a) shall be laid before each House of Parliament by a member of the Queen’s Privy Council for Canada within five sitting days of that House after it is made.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7:

The Minister of Foreign Affairs may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, enter into an agreement with the government of any foreign state respecting the use by the foreign state, for any of the following purposes, of amounts that may be paid out under section 5.6:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the reconstruction of the foreign state adversely affected by a grave breach of international peace and security;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the restoration of international peace and security; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the compensation of victims of a grave breach of international peace and security, gross and systematic human rights violations or acts of significant corruption.

Heading.TitleText: Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law)

Subsection.Text: The definition Minister in section 2 of the Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law) is repealed.
Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: property means any type of property, whether real or personal or immovable or movable, or tangible or intangible or corporeal or incorporeal, and includes money, funds, currency, digital assets and virtual currency. (bien)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 2:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), the Minister of Foreign Affairs is responsible for the administration and enforcement of this Act.

The Governor in Council may, by order, designate one or more Ministers of the Crown to discharge any responsibilities that the Governor in Council may specify with respect to the administration or enforcement of any of the provisions of this Act or any order or regulations made under this Act.

Section.Text: Paragraph 4(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: by order, cause to be seized or restrained in the manner set out in the order any property situated in Canada that is owned — or that is held or controlled, directly or indirectly — by the foreign national.

Section.Text: Section 5 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The following definitions apply in sections 4.2 to 4.4.

Definition.Text: judge means a judge of a superior court of the province where property described in an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b) is situated. (juge)
Minister means the Minister responsible under section 2.1 for the administration of an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b). (ministre)

Subsection.Text: On application by the Minister, a judge must order that the property that is the subject of the application be forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada if the judge determines, based on the evidence presented, that the property

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is described in an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is owned by the foreign national referred to in that order or is held or controlled, directly or indirectly, by that foreign national.

Subsection.Text: Before making the order in relation to the property, the court must require notice to be given to any person who, in the court’s opinion, appears to have an interest in or right to the property, and the court may hear any such person.
The notice must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: be given in the manner that the court directs or that may be specified in the rules of the court;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: specify the period that the court considers reasonable or that may be set out in the rules of the court during which a person may, before the order in relation to the property is made, make an application to the court asserting their interest in or right to the property; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: set out a description of the property.

Subsection.Text: Any person — other than a foreign national described in any of paragraphs 4(2)(a) to (d) — who claims an interest in or right to property that is forfeited to Her Majesty under subsection (1) may, within 30 days after the day on which the property is forfeited, apply in writing to a judge for an order declaring that their interest or right is not affected by the forfeiture, declaring the nature and extent of the interest or right and directing the Minister to pay to the person an amount equal to the value of their interest or right.

Section.Text: If the property that is the subject of a forfeiture order consists of all of the shares of a corporation, the corporation is deemed not to be a Crown corporation as defined in subsection 83(1) of the Financial Administration Act.

After consulting with the Minister of Finance and the Minister of Foreign Affairs, the Minister may — at the times and in the manner, and on any terms and conditions, that the Minister considers appropriate — pay out of the Proceeds Account, as defined in section 2 of the Seized Property Management Act, amounts not exceeding the net proceeds from the disposition of property forfeited under section 4.2, but only to compensate victims of the circumstances described in subsection 4(2).

A copy of each order or regulation made under paragraph 4(1)(a) must be tabled in each House of Parliament within 15 days after it is made. It may be sent to the Clerk of the House if the House is not sitting.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7:

The following persons may assist the Minister in matters relating to the making, administration or enforcement of an order or regulation referred to in subsection 4(1) and, for that purpose, may collect information from and disclose information to each other:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Foreign Affairs;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Finance;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Public Works and Government Services;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the Director of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief of the Communications Security Establishment;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the President of the Canada Border Services Agency; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the Superintendent of Financial Institutions.

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police may assist the Minister in matters related to the making of an order under paragraph 4(1)(b), the seizure or restraint of any property that is the subject of such an order or the making of an application for forfeiture of the property under section 4.2 and, for that purpose, may collect information from and disclose information to the persons referred to in section 7.1.
For greater certainty, nothing in subsection (1) is to be construed as affecting the powers of a peace officer that are conferred under legislation or the common law.

The Minister of Foreign Affairs may require any person to provide to that Minister any information that that Minister believes on reasonable grounds is relevant for the purposes of the making, administration or enforcement of an order or regulation referred to in subsection 4(1).
Every person who is required to provide information under subsection (1) must comply with the requirement within the time and in the form and manner specified by that Minister.

Section.Text: Subsections 8(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A foreign national who is the subject of an order or regulation made under paragraph 4(1)(a) may apply in writing to the Minister to cease being the subject of the order or regulation.

A foreign national whose property is the subject of an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b) may, unless the property is the subject of a forfeiture order, apply at any time in writing to the Minister to request that the property cease being the subject of the order made under that paragraph.

On receipt of the application, the Minister must decide whether there are reasonable grounds to recommend to the Governor in Council that the order or regulation be amended or repealed, as the case may be, so that the applicant or their property ceases to be the subject of it.

Section.Text: Section 13 of the Act is replaced by the following:

All secured and unsecured rights and interests in any property that is the subject of an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b) that are held by a person are entitled to the same ranking that they would have been entitled to had the order not been made, unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is a foreign national described in any of paragraphs 4(2)(a) to (d); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the property is forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada under section 4.2.

Section.Text: Any costs incurred by or on behalf of Her Majesty in right of Canada in relation to the seizure or restraint of property under an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b) or the disposal of property forfeited under section 4.2 are the liability of the owner of the property and constitute a debt due to Her Majesty in right of Canada that may be recovered in any court of competent jurisdiction.

The Minister of Foreign Affairs may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, enter into an agreement with a person respecting the use by the person, for the compensation of victims of the circumstances described in subsection 4(2), of amounts that may be paid out under section 4.4.
Subsection 13(3) of the Seized Property Management Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: amounts paid under section 5.6 of the Special Economic Measures Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: amounts paid under section 4.4 of the Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law).

Provision.Text: Vaping products that are vaping devices that contain vaping substances or that are vaping substances in immediate containers: for each vaping device or immediate container of vaping substance

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: if the vaping substance is in liquid form, the amount equal to the total of

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: for the first 10 millilitres of vaping substance in the vaping device or immediate container, $1.00 per 2 millilitres of vaping substance or fraction thereof, and

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: for any additional amount of vaping substance in the vaping device or immediate container, $1.00 per 10 millilitres of vaping substance or fraction thereof; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: if the vaping substance is in solid form, the amount equal to the total of

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: for the first 10 grams of vaping substance in the vaping device or immediate container, $1.00 per 2 grams of vaping substance or fraction thereof, and

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: for any additional amount of vaping substance in the vaping device or immediate container, $1.00 per 10 grams of vaping substance or fraction thereof.
Vaping products that are vaping substances not in any vaping device or immediate container:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: if the vaping substance is in liquid form, the amount equal to the total of

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: for the first 10 millilitres of vaping substance, $1.00 per 2 millilitres of vaping substance or fraction thereof, and

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: for any additional amount of vaping substance, $1.00 per 10 millilitres of vaping substance or fraction thereof; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: if the vaping substance is in solid form, the amount equal to the total of

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: for the first 10 grams of vaping substance, $1.00 per 2 grams of vaping substance or fraction thereof, and

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: for any additional amount of vaping substance, $1.00 per 10 grams of vaping substance or fraction thereof.

North Caribou Lake First Nation (Round Lake First Nation)
